## PAGE 06347: Supported SQL statements

- page_id: `249567356`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567356/Supported+SQL+statements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:28:02.202+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > Database support > Database code engineering
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section lists the SQL statements supported in the Cameo Data Modeler plugin. They are parsed and mapped into model constructs.

The following table provides SQL2 SQL schema statements and their support status in the Cameo Data Modeler plugin**. Yes**indicates that a statement can be generated into a DLL script from model constructs and reverse engineered from a script into model constructs.

| SQL schema statement | Supported | (Yes/No) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SQL schema definition statement | Schema definition | Yes |
| Table definition | Yes |  |
| View definition | Yes |  |
| Alter table statement | Yes |  |
| Grant statement | No |  |
| Domain definition | No |  |
| Assertion definition | No |  |
| Character set definition | No |  |
| Collation definition | No |  |
| Translation definition | No |  |
| SQL schema manipulation statement | Drop table statement | Yes |
| Drop view statement | Yes |  |
| Revoke statement | No |  |
| Alter domain statement | No |  |
| Drop assertion statement | No |  |
| Drop domain statement | No |  |
| Drop character set statement | No |  |
| Drop collation statement | No |  |
| Drop translation statement | No |  |

Some SQL schema statements (e.g. schema definition, table definition) allow implicit catalog names and unqualified schema names. In addition to SQL schema statements, the following SQL session statements must be supported:

- Set catalog statement - sets the current default catalog name.
- Set schema statement - sets the current default unqualified schema name.

Cameo Data Modeler supports the following widely used dialect statements that are not part of SQL2:

- Database definition statement (CREATE DATABASE) that creates a database
- Index statements (CREATE INDEX, DROP INDEX) that create or remove an index on a table
- Trigger statements (CREATE TRIGGER, DROP TRIGGER) that create or remove trigger(s) on a table

The following table provides details on mapping on the supported SQL schema manipulation statements into SQL constructs.

| DDL Statement or Concept | Action, model Item | Description | Visible |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Alter table statement | Modify class | Elements: table name and alter table action. Alter table action – one of: add column, add table constraint, alter column, drop table constraint, drop column. | Yes |
| Add column definition | Define attribute | Elements: column definition. | Yes |
| Add table constraint definition | Define method | Elements: table constraint definition. | Yes |
| Alter column definition | Modify attribute | Elements: mandatory column name, default clause (for add default statement only). | Yes |
| Drop table constraint definition | Delete method | Elements: constraint name, drop behavior | Yes |
| Drop column definition | Delete attribute | Elements: column name, drop behavior | Yes |
| Drop schema statement | Delete package | Elements: schema name, drop behavior | Yes |
| Drop table statement | Delete class | Elements: table name, drop behavior | Yes |
| Drop view statement | Delete class | Elements: table name, drop behavior | Yes |
| Drop behavior | Action property | Modifiers: CASCADE, RESTRICT | No |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization Structure Diagram' space='']

- space_key: `MT`

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the migration to UAF, the Supports relationships between Operational Activities and Services are created. The relationships are displayed in the following matrices:

UAF: Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

DoDAF: SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

NAF: NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping

NAF4: S4 Services to Operational Activities Mapping

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 06351: Suspect Links

- page_id: `243969653`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969653/Suspect+Links
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:01:59.760+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Working on a server project requires up-to-date knowledge of all relevant project changes. Thus, we introduce a new set of validation rules for suspect link analysis. These rules help you track changes in Requirements that are linked to design elements (such as SysML v1 Blocks, Test Cases, Use Cases, etc.) by Satisfy, Derive, Refine or Verify relationships, and stay informed about unlinked and deprecated Requirements in the model.

700500

INLINE

The following topics contain more information on Requirements tracking:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Suspect links tracking options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Suspect links tracking options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes' space='']

You can visit the page[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Suspect links semantics' space='']to learn more about the topic.

You can also create your own suspect links customization to keep track of the changes in the elements of the specified type. To learn more, read the following topic:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing suspect links tracking' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969676 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06352: Suspect links semantics

- page_id: `243969676`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969676/Suspect+links+semantics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:35.136+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Suspect Links
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Enabling suspect links tracking applies the *<<track>>* stereotype to the relationships. It is applied to the relationships automatically right after enabling the suspect links tracking, changing the tracking scope, or creating a new relationship (in the case when suspect links are already enabled) if the following criteria are satisfied:

- The relationship type is Satisfy, Verify, Refine, or deriveReq. If custom suspect links tracking is created, the relationship type, direction, source, and target element types should be as defined in the configuration.
- The relationship itself and the relationship's source and target elements are under the specified tracking scope.

The stereotype contains *fingerprint* and *version* tags:

- fingerprint: is used to indicate that changes occurred at the end of a relationship. In other words, the fingerprint is used to highlight that relationship is suspected.
- version: is used to get the Teamwork Cloud server project version, which should be compared to the latest in order to identify what changes occurred in the linked element . In other words, the version is used when the action "Show Changes" is performed on a suspected link.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Upon enabling suspect links tracking, the stereotype *<<track>>*, which contains fingerprint and version tags, is applied tothe relationship 'Satisfy'.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Enabling suspect links tracking applies the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">&lt;&lt;track&gt;&gt;</em> stereotype to the relationships. It is applied to the relationships automatically right after enabling the suspect links tracking, changing the tracking scope, or creating a new relationship (in the case when suspect links are already enabled) if the following criteria are satisfied:</p><ul><li>The relationship type is Satisfy, Verify, Refine, or deriveReq. If custom suspect links tracking is created, the relationship type, direction, source, and target element types should be as defined in the configuration.</li><li>The relationship itself and the relationship's source and target elements are under the specified tracking scope.</li></ul><p>The stereotype contains <em>fingerprint</em> and <em>version</em> tags:</p><ul><li>fingerprint: is used to indicate<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> that changes occurred at the end of a relationship. In other words, the fingerprint is used to highlight that relationship is suspected.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">version: </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">is used to get the Teamwork Cloud server project version, which should be compared to the latest in order to identify what changes occurred in the linked element</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In other words, t</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">he version is used when the action &quot;Show Changes&quot; is performed on a suspected link.</span></span></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="suspect_links_semantics.png" /></ac:image> <br />Upon enabling suspect links tracking, the stereotype <em>&lt;&lt;track&gt;&gt;</em>, which contains fingerprint and version tags, <span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">is applied to </span><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">the relationship 'Satisfy'</span>.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969668 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06353: Suspect links tracking options

- page_id: `243969668`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969668/Suspect+links+tracking+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:34.901+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Suspect Links
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

767157416

767157437

767157414

**On this page**

**3**

767157427

##### Tracking Deprecated Requirements

Suspect links analysis allows tracking deprecated Requirements. This is useful when you are working with Requirements and some of them are no longer used. In that case, apply the *«deprecated»* stereotype to mark the Requirements of this type. All deprecated Requirements are provided in the **Validation Results** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To check whether deprecated Requirements are tracked

- In the main menu, go to Analyze > Suspect Links and select Options . If the Track Deprecated Elements option is set to true , tracking deprecated elements is enabled. [ATTACHMENT filename='track_deprecated_elements_option.png']

##### Tracking Unlinked Requirements

Suspect links analysis allows tracking unlinked Requirements. This is useful when you're working with structure elements and you need to know which Requirements remain unsatisfied.

To check whether unlinked Requirements are satisfied

1. In the main toolbar, go to Analyze > Suspect Links and select Options .
2. Click the arrow next to the Track Unlinked Requirements option and choose: a) All – if you want to track all the unlinked Requirements b) Excluding Owning – if you want to track only those unlinked Requirements, that have no sub-Requirements c) None – if you want to disable tracking unlinked Requirements. [ATTACHMENT filename='excluding_owning.png']
3. Click OK .

All unlinked Requirements are provided in the **Validation Results** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Tracking Links in Main Project Only

You can limit the suspect links analysis scope to links contained in the main project only. If the option is enabled, used projects are excluded from the tracking scope.

To track links in the main project only

- In the main menu, go to Analyze > Suspect Links and select Options . If the Track Links in Main Project Only option is set to true , links are tracked in the main project only. [ATTACHMENT filename='track_links_in_main_project_only.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562733 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06354: SV-1 Systems Interface Description

- page_id: `249562733`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562733/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:51.044+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-1-systems-interface-description', 'sv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

404622161

404622164

404622162

**Description**

The SV-1 addresses the composition and interaction of systems. For DoDAF V2.0, the SV-1 incorporates the human elements as types of performers- Organizations and Personnel Types.

The SV-1 links together the operational and systems architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']. A SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description (i.e., in a “To-Be” architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” architecture, the OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key resource flows to non-technical stakeholders.

A system resource flow is a simplified representation of a pathway or network pattern, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e., a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all system resource flows between systems that are of interest. Note that resource flows between systems may be further specified in detail in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''].

Sub-system assemblies may be identified in SV-1 to any level (i.e., depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g., Platforms) at which resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Activities and Locations that utilize those resources. In many cases, an Operational Activity and Locations depicted in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description model may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in SV-1.

The intended usage of the SV-1 includes:

- Definition of system concepts.
- Definition of system options.
- System resource flow requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability and performer definition).

The SV-1 is used in two complementary ways:

- Describe the resource flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.
- Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performersoriginally specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performerto another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='']. Resource Flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='']).

The system Functions performed by the performersare specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''], but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

404622159

## PAGE 06355: SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description

- page_id: `249562741`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562741/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Internal+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:41.369+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-1-systems-interface-internal-description', 'sv-1-internal', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

404838096

404838098

404838097

**Description**

The SV-1 addresses the internal composition and interaction of systems.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performers originally specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performer to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='']. Resource flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='']).

Thesystem Functions performed by the Nodesare specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''], but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

404838095

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='MED']

In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.</p><p>An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performer to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Resource flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</p><p>The<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> system Functions</span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performed</span> by the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Nodes </span>are specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4 System Functionality Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.</p><p>An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3959acf7-850d-4cf6-b541-1391f37bafd1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">404838095</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Port" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Connector" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SV-1 Internal diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in SV-1 Internal diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 06356: SV-10a Resource Parametric

- page_id: `249563025`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563025/SV-10a+Resource+Parametric
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:01.778+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

497880528

497880530

497880529

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Capability Configuration. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

497880527

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e0817d3-1533-46ca-b368-1e58ee00302d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497880527</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd54d075-b77d-4ab9-805b-ab499fd6c47c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497306340</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c7c89af-5e28-4882-8d9a-67e9b6029458"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497306342</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf3f6ff5-fe94-46e8-bdbe-d483b09986aa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497306341</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint block]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to constrain the properties of another Capability Configuration. It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d5628d8-8f8e-4a58-873b-10807d0c2d0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497306338</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562821 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06358: SV-10a Systems Rules Model

- page_id: `249562821`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562821/SV-10a+Systems+Rules+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:43.685+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10a-systems-rules-model', 'sv-10a', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408324973

408324976

408324974

**Description**

The SV-10a specifies functional and nonfunctional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e., the structural and behavioral elements of the Systems Viewpoint).

The SV-10a DoDAF-described Model describes constraints on the resources, functions, data, and ports that make up the SV. The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e., non-functional).

The intended usage of the SV-10a includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

Implementation

SV-10a can be represented using:

- SV-10a table. [ATTACHMENT filename='sv_10a.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10a Systems Parametric' space=''] .
- SV-10a spreadsheet report.

In contrast to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space=''], SV-10a focuses on physical and data constraints rather than business rules.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

408324971

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562829 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06359: SV-10b Systems State Transition Description

- page_id: `249562829`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562829/SV-10b+Systems+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-08-27T08:30:47.805+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10b-systems-state-transition-description', 'sv-10b', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resourceor system function response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. The SV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the functions. Alternatively, SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of system functions with respect to a specific resource.

The intended usage of the SV-10b includes:

- Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of functions described in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. However, the relationship between the actions included in SV-10b and the functions in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] depends on the purposes of the architecture and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']. SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.

Depending upon the architecture project’s needs, the SV-10b may be used separately or in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description' space=''].

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> or system function</span> response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. The SV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the functions. Alternatively, SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of system functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-10b includes:</p><ul><li data-uuid="ac06112c-ebdd-4c0d-94e7-df03c805edf9">Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).</li><li data-uuid="eda93feb-6a0c-49a6-b15a-38de62fc94f4">Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" ac:alt="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-10b_sys_state_transit_desc.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The SV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of functions described in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. However, the relationship between the actions included in SV-10b and the functions in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link> depends on the purposes of the architecture and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>Depending upon the architecture project’s needs, the SV-10b may be used separately or in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="a141d2dd-583e-48e3-9f86-b50f3d84ae01"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="State" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="e2df8941-7305-43a5-a30a-9b7f94743ec6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Artifact" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9a52e886-9a8d-49f3-83e4-58ff7900f427"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Software" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="be7b5231-2714-41ec-890e-df126a26a4a7"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Capability Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="df43132f-9b89-404b-ac93-ce6428e2e201"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Organization" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="54471024-edec-485c-87ef-86d1ff94dbe0"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Post" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="7fc137f1-63cf-4b1a-a416-598f1225d1ea"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562832 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06360: SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `249562832`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562832/SV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:44.034+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10c-systems-event-trace-description', 'sv-10c', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408519862

408519864

408519863

**Description**

The SV-10c provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or system port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of the SV-10c includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10b Systems State Transition Description' space=''] to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect resource flows in a SV-10c may be related with resource flows (the interactions in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='']), resource flows (the data in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='']) and entities (in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='']) modeled in other models.

408519861

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Message' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or system port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-10c includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of resource events impacting operation.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of non-functional system requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" ac:alt="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-10c_sys_event_trace_desc.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" /></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect resource flows in a SV-10c may be related with resource flows (the interactions in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3 Systems- Systems Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), resource flows (the data in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /></ac:link>) and entities (in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="DIV-3 Physical Data Model" /></ac:link>) modeled in other models.</p><p><br /></p><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd311ea2-b24d-4037-8781-e1b9e6789282"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408519861</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Message" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2e3c6f87-8b7d-41b6-915c-e381a25cebce" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562837 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06361: SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `249562837`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562837/SV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:44.222+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p>
````

- page_id: `249562755`
- space_key: `MT`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

405340330

405340333

405340331

**Description**

A SV-2 specifies the internal system resource flows between systems and may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Any protocol referred to in a SV-2 diagram needs to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space=''].

405340329

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562749 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06363: SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `249562749`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562749/SV-2+Systems+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:51.586+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-2-systems-resource-flow-description', 'sv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

404994356

404994358

404994357

**Description**

A SV-2 specifies the system resource flows between systems and may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

A SV-2 described Model is used to give a precise specification of a connection between systems. This may be an existing connection, or a specification for a connection that is to be made.

The intended usage of the SV-2 includes:

- Resource flow specification.

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

Any protocol referred to in a SV-2 diagram needs to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space=''].

404994355

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562763 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06364: SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix

- page_id: `249562763`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562763/SV-3+Systems-Systems+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:08.520+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-3-systems-systems-matrix', 'sv-3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

405463656

405463658

405463657

**Description**

A SV-3 enables a quick overview of all the system resource interactions specified in one or more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] models. The SV-3 provides a tabular summary of the system interactions specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] model for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).

The SV-3 can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of groups of system pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose.

The intended usage of the SV-3 includes:

- Summarizing system resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SV-3 can be represented using a SV-3 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] concentrates on system resources and their interactions, and these are summarized in a SV-3.

405463655

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562781 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06365: SV-4 Functionality Description BPD

- page_id: `249562781`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562781/SV-4+Functionality+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:42.571+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562771 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06366: SV-4 Systems Functionality Description

- page_id: `249562771`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562771/SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:52.516+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-4-systems-functionality-description', 'sv-4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

405595921

405595923

405595922

**Description**

The SV-4 addresses human and system functionality.

The primary purposes of SV-4 are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The Systems Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of functions to resources.
- Flow of resources between functions.

The SV-4 is the Systems Viewpoint model counterpart to the OV-5b Activity Model of the Operational Viewpoint.

The intended usage of the SV-4 includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

SV-4 can be represented using:

- SV-4 diagram for System Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='sv_4.png']
- SV-4 diagram for System Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

The SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] (in the same way that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='']OV-5b Operational Activity Model is the behavioral counterpart to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description).

405595920

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Architecture' space='MED']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Action' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Control Flow' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Object Flow' space='MED']

## PAGE 06367: SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `249562783`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562783/SV-5a+Operational+Activity+to+Systems+Function+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:52.909+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-5a', 'sv-5a-operational-activity-to-systems-function-traceability-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

406176080

406176083

406176082

**Description**

The SV-5a addresses the linkage between systemfunctions described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] and Operational Activities specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='']. The SV-5a depicts the mapping of systemfunctions and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5a identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposefulaction performed by a systemor solution.

During requirements definition, the SV-5a plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SV-5a includes:

- Tracing functional system requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SV-5a can be represented using a SV-5a diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. Systemfunctions are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term system function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing; both activities and functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Function is a question of what and how.

The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A system function is specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, SV-5a is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the OV-5a with the physical specification of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']. System functions can be carried out by Functional Resources(systems, performers executing activities, and performers).

406176079

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implements' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='']

The SV-5a depicts the mapping of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">system </span>functions and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5a identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> action performed by a system </span>or solution.</p><p>During requirements definition, the SV-5a plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-5a includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing functional system requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-5a can be represented using a SV-5a diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">System </span>functions are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" ac:alt="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-5a.png" /></ac:image></p><p>DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">system</span> function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing; both activities and functions are tasks that are <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Function is a question of what and how.</span></p><p>The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">system</span> function is specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, SV-5a is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the OV-5a with the physical specification of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">System</span> functions can be carried out by Functional Resources<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> (systems, performers executing activities, and performers).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cca833aa-6a8b-429c-b0f9-15449df37733"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">406176079</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Implements" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f8c1b6a1-6e44-4d11-ae6b-2dccc6fde77c" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562788 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06368: SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `249562788`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562788/SV-5b+Operational+Activity+to+Systems+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:53.235+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-5b-operational-activity-to-systems-traceability-matrix', 'sv-5b', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

407600326

407600328

407600327

**Description**

The SV-5b addresses the linkage between described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] and Operational Activities specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='']. The SV-5b depicts the mapping of systems and, optionally, the capabilities and nodes that provide them to operational activities.The SV-5b identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

During requirements definition, the SV-5b plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SV-5b includes:

- Tracing system requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SV-5b can be represented using a SV-5b diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Resourcesare used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-5b is generally presented as a matrix of the relationship between systemresourcesand activities and can be a summary of the Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space='']). The SV-5b can show requirements traceability with Operational Activities on one axis of a matrix, the System Functions on the other axis, and with an X, date, or phase in the intersecting cells, where appropriate.

407600325

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']

The SV-5b can show requirements traceability with Operational Activities on one axis of a matrix, the System Functions on the other axis, and with an X, date, or phase in the intersecting cells, where appropriate.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3cb982c-f8e0-44e7-af22-f7c23654069e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">407600325</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Artifact" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Is Capable To Perform" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Implements" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8d6e176d-0784-4323-97c7-32722b44ca32" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 06369: SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `249562794`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562794/SV-6+Systems+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:53.592+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-6-systems-resource-flow-matrix', 'sv-6', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

407725747

407725749

407725748

**Description**

The SV-6 specifies the characteristics of the system resource flows exchanged between system resourceswith emphasis on resources crossing the system boundary.

The SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system resource flow and the system resource flow content in a tabular format.

The intended usage of the SV-6 includes:

- Detailed definition of resource flows.

**Implementation**

SV-6 can be represented using:

- SV-6 matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='sv_6.png']
- SV-6 role-based matrix. A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles (resource usages). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- SV-6 spreadsheet report.

The SV-6 is the physical equivalent of the logical [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] table and provides detailed information on the system connections which implement the resource flow exchanges specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''].

407725746

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562801 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06370: SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix

- page_id: `249562801`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562801/SV-7+Systems+Measures+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:53.943+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-7-systems-measures-matrix', 'sv-7', 'sv-7-actual-measures-table', 'sv-7-typical-measures-table', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

407909009

407909011

407909010

**Description**

The SV-7 depicts the measures (metrics) of resources. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the resources in the SV-1.

The intended usage of the SV-7 includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

SV-7 can be represented using:

- SV-7 Typical measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='sv_7_typical.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='']
- SV-7 Actual measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='sv_7_actual.png']
- SV-7 actual measures spreadsheet report

The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] by depicting the characteristics of the Systems and other Resources shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='']. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''] by depicting the characteristics of the Systems shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''].

407909008

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2ec51bf-03ec-497f-b9af-6a178e772641"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">407909009</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d5c4779-f1e8-4c9d-a04f-533d9d0511b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">407909011</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c2c5e75-62a6-40c7-905b-4e623fc167bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">407909010</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-7 depicts the measures (metrics) of resources. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the resources in the SV-1.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-7 includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).</li><li>Identification of non-functional requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-7 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SV-7 Typical measures table<br /><ac:image ac:title="SV-7 Systems Typical Measures Matrix" ac:alt="SV-7 Systems Typical Measures Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv_7_typical.png" /></ac:image></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Hierarchical Typical Measurements table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Hierarchical Actual Measurements table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>SV-7 Actual measures table<br /><ac:image ac:title="SV-7 Systems Actual Measures Matrix" ac:alt="SV-7 Systems Actual Measures Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv_7_actual.png" /></ac:image></li><li>SV-7 actual measures spreadsheet report</li></ul><p>The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by depicting the characteristics of the Systems and other Resources shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by depicting the characteristics of the Systems shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1dddee73-97ef-4d10-b543-ff5b21bf117d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">407909008</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Artifact" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Software" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Capability Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Organization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Post" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Measurement Set" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Measurement Set" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Measurement" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Measurement" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Typical Measurements table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Actual Measurements table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table" document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562805 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06371: SV-8 Systems Evolution Description

- page_id: `249562805`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562805/SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:43.260+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-8-systems-evolution-description', 'sv-8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408089834

408089836

408089835

**Description**

The SV-8 presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (systems), describing how they change over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

The intended usage of the SV-8 includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The SV-8 view can be represented using a SV-8 Systems Evolution Description diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-8 model shows the structure of resources modeled in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='']. The changes depicted in the SV-8 are derived from the project milestones that are shown in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='']. When the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space=''] is used for capability acquisition projects, there is likely to be a close relationship between these two models.

408089833

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Version Of Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-8 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Resources from SV-8 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Version of Configurations from SV-8 diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562814 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06372: SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast

- page_id: `249562814`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562814/SV-9+Systems+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:43.513+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-9-systems-technology-and-skills-forecast', 'sv-9', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408231854

408231856

408231855

**Description**

The SV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills as well as the expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space=''] milestones and linked to capability phases.

The SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture.

The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:

- Emerging capabilities.
- Industry trends.
- Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software systems.
- Current and possible future skills.

In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and systems, the DoDAF-described Model SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals.

The intended usage of the SV-9 includes:

- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR Trends Analysis.
- Recruitment Planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.

**Implementation**

SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-9 DoDAF-described Model forecasts relates to the Standards Profile ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='']) in that a timed forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a resource. Similarly, SV-9 forecasts relate to the Standards Forecasts ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space='']) in that a certain standard may be adopted depending on a certain technology or skill becoming available (e.g., the availability of Java Script may influence the decision to adopt a new HTML standard).

408231853

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-9 table' space='']

Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills as well as the expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-8]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> milestones and linked to capability phases.</p><p>The SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture.</p><p>The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:</p><ul><li>Emerging capabilities.</li><li>Industry trends.</li><li>Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software systems.</li><li>Current and possible future skills.</li></ul><p>In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and systems, the DoDAF-described Model SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-9 includes:</p><ul><li>Forecasting technology readiness against time.</li><li>HR Trends Analysis.</li><li>Recruitment Planning.</li><li>Planning technology insertion.</li><li>Input to options analysis.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast" ac:alt="SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv_9.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The SV-9 DoDAF-described Model forecasts relates to the Standards Profile (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="StdV-1 Standards Profile" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StdV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) in that a timed forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a resource. Similarly, SV-9 forecasts relate to the Standards Forecasts (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StdV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) in that a certain standard may be adopted depending on a certain technology or skill becoming available (e.g., the availability of Java Script may influence the decision to adopt a new HTML standard).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b8e3266-2422-4f49-9b7c-f8d08529e5f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408231853</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Forecast" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Artifact" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Software" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Capability Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Organization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Post" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Protocol" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Standard" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SV-9 table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565590 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06373: SvcV diagrams migration

- page_id: `249565590`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565590/SvcV+diagrams+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:38.141+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Issue**

All Service Access elements are migrated to Capability Configuration elements. That affects all diagrams in the Services viewpoint (SvcV). For resources modelingin SV diagrams, use Capability Configuration elements, but for services modeling in SvcV diagrams, the Capability Configurations, Resource Roles, all relationships, and Ports must be converted manually.

**Solution**

For smooth Capability Configurations, Resource Roles, all relationships, and Ports manually conversion, do the following:

truenone

##### Convert Capability Configurations to Services

To convert Capability Configuration to Service

1. Right-click the single or multiple Capability Configurations.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choose the Service element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert Resource Roles to Service Roles

If Capability Configuration has a Resource Role, it must be converted to Service Role manually.

To convert Resource Role to Service Role

1. Right-click the single or multiple Resource Roles.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choose the Service Role element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert relationships between Service Roles to Service Connectors

All existing relationships between already converted Service Roles must be converted to Service Connector manually.

To convert relationships between Service Role to Service Connector

1. Right-click the single or multiple relationships between ServiceRoles.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choosethe Service Connectorelement.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert Ports to Service Ports

If Service has Ports, they must be converted to Service Ports manually.

To convert Port to Service Port

1. Right-click the single or multiple Ports.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choosethe Service Port element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Issue</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">All Service Access elements are migrated to Capability Configuration elements. That affects all diagrams in the Services viewpoint (<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">SvcV</span>).
````
