<strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose the Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span> element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Convert relationships <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">between Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span></span></span>s to Service Connectors<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">All existing relationships between already converted Service Roles must be converted to Service Connector manually.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To convert relationships between Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span></span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">to Service Connector</span><br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the single or multiple <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">relationships <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">between Service<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span></span></span>s</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> the Service Connector </span>element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Convert Ports to Service Ports</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If Service has Ports, they must be converted to Service Ports manually.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To convert Port <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">to Service Port</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the single or multiple <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Ports</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Service Port</span></span> </span> element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565553 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06374: SvcV diagrams migration in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `249565553`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565553/SvcV+diagrams+migration+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:36.979+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Issue**

All Service Access elements are migrated to Resource Service elements. That affects diagrams in Services viewpoint (SvcV). For resources modelingin SV diagrams, use Resource Service elements, but for services modeling in SvcV diagrams, the Resource Service, Resource Roles, all relationships, and Ports must be converted manually.

**Solution**

For a smooth Capability Configurations, Resource Roles, all relationships, and Ports manually conversion, do the following:

truenone

##### Convert Resource Service to Service

To convert Resource Service to Service

1. Right-click the single or multiple Resource Service.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choose Service element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert Resource Roles to Service Roles

If Resource Service has a Resource Role, it must be converted to Service Role manually.

To convert Resource Role to Service Role

1. Right-click the single or multiple Resource Roles.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choose Service Role element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert relationships between Service Roles to Service Connectors

All existing relationships between already converted Service Roles, must be converted to Service Connector manually.

To convert relationships between Service Role to Service Connector

1. Right-click the single or multiple relationships between Service Roles.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, chooseService Connector element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert ports to Service Ports

If Service has ports, their must be converted to Service Ports manually.

To convert port to Service Port

1. Right-click the single or multiple ports.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, chooseService Port element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span> element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Convert relationships <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">between Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span></span></span>s to Service Connectors<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">All existing relationships between already converted Service Roles, must be converted to Service Connector manually.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To convert relationships between Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span></span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">to Service Connector</span><br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the single or multiple <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">relationships <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">between Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span></span></span>s</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> Service Connector </span> element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Convert ports to Service Ports</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If Service has ports, their must be converted to Service Ports manually.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To convert port <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">to Service Port</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the single or multiple p<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">orts</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Service Port</span></span> </span> element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562639 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06375: SvcV-1 Services Context Description

- page_id: `249562639`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562639/SvcV-1+Services+Context+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:38.185+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-1-services-context-description', 'svcv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394841328

394841330

394841329

**Description**

The SvcV-1 addresses the composition and interaction of services. For DoDAF V2.0, SvcV-1 incorporates human elements as types ofperformers - Organizations and Personnel Types.

The SvcV-1 links together the operational and services architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description. A SvcV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] (i.e., in a “To-Be” Architectural Description), and so there may be many alternative SvcV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” Architectural Description, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SvcV-1 to allow communication of key Resource Flows to non-technical stakeholders.

The intended usage of the SvcV-1 includes:

- Definition of service concepts.
- Definition of service options.
- Service Resource Flow requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- Service integration management.
- Operational planning (capability and performer definition).

The SvcV-1 is used in two complementary ways:

- Describe the Resource Flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.
- Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.

**Implementation**

SvcV-1 can be represented using a SvcV-1 diagram which is based on the UML class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-1 links together the operational and services architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']. A SvcV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] (i.e., in a “To-Be” Architectural Description), and so there may be many alternative SvcV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in annus-Is” Architectural Description, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SvcV-1 to allow communication of key Resource Flows to non-technical stakeholders.

Some Resources can carry out service functions (activities) as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''] models and these functions can optionally be overlaid on a SvcV-1.

The SvcV-1 depicts all Resource Flows between resources that are of interest. Note that Resource Flows between resources may be further specified in detail in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space=''] model.

394841327

## PAGE 06376: SvcV-10a Services Parametric

- page_id: `249562718`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562718/SvcV-10a+Services+Parametric
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:50.571+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-10a Services Rules Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

497450681

497450683

497450682

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Service Specification. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

497450680

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a693f2ce-e38f-4839-af40-e1a65d49e6b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497450680</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562712 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06377: SvcV-10a Services Rules Model

- page_id: `249562712`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562712/SvcV-10a+Services+Rules+Model
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-27T08:38:06.695+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10a-services-rules-model', 'svcv-10a', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-10a is to specify functional and nonfunctional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (that is, the structural and behavioral elements of the Services Model).

The SvcV-10a describes constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Service Model.

The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (that is, non-functional).

The intended usage of the SvcV-10a includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10a can be represented using:

- SvcV-10a table. [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_10a.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10a Services Parametric' space=''] .
- SvcV-10a spreadsheet report.

In contrast to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space=''], the SvcV-10a focuses physical and data constraints rather than business rules.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-10a table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10a Services Parametric' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-10a is to specify functional and nonfunctional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (that is, the structural and behavioral elements of the Services Model).</p><p>The SvcV-10a describes constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Service Model.</p><p>The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (that is, non-functional).</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10a includes:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b4f4d99b-a6fd-4a38-91ac-bf6939ecb053">Definition of implementation logic.</li><li data-uuid="5ef395c5-d744-44a9-8881-ba441112ecef">Identification of resource constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-10a can be represented using:</p><ul><li data-uuid="8203d04d-2194-4db1-ac1a-b80a01a58cc6">SvcV-10a table.<br /><ac:image ac:title="SvcV-10a Services Rules Model" ac:alt="SvcV-10a Services Rules Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="svcv_10a.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="24f63ce0-7b0b-47aa-990b-76dba0480980"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10a Services Parametric" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-10a Services Parametric diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="4644553f-f044-4693-a37b-cb64d68efb9c">SvcV-10a spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>In contrast to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-6a Operational Rules Model" /></ac:link>, the SvcV-10a focuses physical and data constraints rather than business rules.</p><p><strong>Constraint owner</strong></p><p>You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the<span> </span><strong>Project Options</strong>.</p><p>To select a constraint owner</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d5fdd2a5-db03-460d-a85f-2de945a89669">In the main menu, click<span> </span><strong>Options</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Project</strong>. The<span> </span><strong>Project Options</strong><span> </span>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="727ba4af-59bd-4ab3-a5a7-80611f655fe1">Go to<span> </span><strong>General</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>UAF</strong>, and in the<span> </span><strong>Default Constraint Owners</strong><span> </span>property group, choose a needed owner.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="4549a93e-1bc3-446a-84b7-c38e92437569"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="a625dda5-80e4-4a0f-9809-74e61f5837fe"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="97d64557-0f2b-4683-85f4-9b7ea1cde9af"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-10a table" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="76b0a65c-4006-4081-b00d-ab5c4e31795e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10a Services Parametric" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562720 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06378: SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description

- page_id: `249562720`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562720/SvcV-10b+Services+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-08-27T08:31:46.005+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10b-services-state-transition-description', 'svcv-10b', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource (or function) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description. SvcV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the service functions. Alternatively, SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single service function, or the sequencing of service functions with respect to a specific resource.

The intended usage of the SvcV-10b includes:

- Definition of states, events, and state transitions (behavioral modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of service functions described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']. However, the relationship between the actions included in SvcV-10b and the functions in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''] depends on the purposes of the Architectural Description and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']. SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource (or function) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description. SvcV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the service functions. Alternatively, SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single service function, or the sequencing of service functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10b includes:</p><ul><li data-uuid="91054eed-7816-45a5-86b5-76db0393ff70">Definition of states, events, and state transitions (behavioral modeling).</li><li data-uuid="da2cb3a6-030c-4a9f-8293-592441929227">Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" ac:alt="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="svcv_10b.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The SvcV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of service functions described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. However, the relationship between the actions included in <span class="confluence-link">SvcV-10b</span> and the functions in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> depends on the purposes of the Architectural Description and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="c4270c12-6480-4092-8397-40f1db12709a"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="State" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="2498da2e-0df2-4909-8bd0-a1813942d663"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562723 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06379: SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `249562723`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562723/SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-27T08:36:39.663+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10c-services-event-trace-description', 'svcv-10c', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-10c provides a time ordered examination of the interactions between services functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The SvcV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of service functions and service data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or Service Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of the SvcV-10c includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description' space=''] to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SvcV-10c model may be related, in modeling terms, with Resource Flows (interactions, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix' space='']), Resource Flows (data, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']) and information elements (in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='']) modeled in other models.

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Message' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-10c provides a time ordered examination of the interactions between services functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>The SvcV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of service functions and service data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or Service Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10c includes:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3d1d7c1c-2bf8-464a-aab3-0f1698e560b1">Analysis of resource events impacting operation.</li><li data-uuid="e4292aad-7f71-4814-b563-d246c2f9b0be">Behavioral analysis.</li><li data-uuid="b86fe7f3-5928-45c3-a064-43a640e66934">Identification of non-functional system requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" ac:alt="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="svcv_10c.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The SvcV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" /></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SvcV-10c model may be related, in modeling terms, with Resource Flows (interactions, in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix" /></ac:link>), Resource Flows (data, in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> </span>) and information elements (in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="DIV-3 Physical Data Model" /></ac:link>) modeled in other models.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="3189a1bc-ee87-4961-b8cd-45bbc3d88652"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="84fc3438-7e7b-4912-b30e-a0c4a2d8bd14"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="f06e2cca-f0a3-478b-b2ee-b1cbc918aee6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Interface" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="a878f9b8-0e9f-4552-8064-25ca21716372"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Message" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="784122c8-fddd-4ce9-982d-eebd8198b7eb"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Method" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="9b9f0db2-913e-4a23-aeb8-9a653c4ee891"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562729 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06380: SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `249562729`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562729/SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:40.735+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562645 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06381: SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `249562645`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562645/SvcV-2+Services+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:38.348+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-2-services-resource-flow-description', 'svcv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

395159322

395159324

395159323

**Description**

A SvcV-2 specifies the Resource Flows between Service Specificationsand may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

A SvcV-2 DoDAF-described Model is used to give a precise specification of a connection between Service Specifications. This may be an existing connection or a specification of a connection that is to be made for a future connection.

The intended usage of the SvcV-2 includes:

- Resource Flow specification.

**Implementation**

SvcV-2 can be represented using:

- SvcV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SvcV-2 Internal Block diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Any protocol referred to in a SvcV-2 diagram needs be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='']StdV-1 Standards Profile.

395159321

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562653 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06382: SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix

- page_id: `249562653`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562653/SvcV-3a+Systems-Services+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:38.593+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-3a-systems-services-matrix', 'svcv-3a', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

395570067

395570069

395570068

**Description**

A SvcV-3a enables a quick overview of all the system-to-service resource interactions specified in one or more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''] models. The SvcV-3a provides a tabular summary of the system and services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. This model can be useful in support existing systems that are transitioning to provide services. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).

The SvcV-3a can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of system-to-service interactions in context with the architecture’s purpose.

The intended usage of the SvcV-3a includes:

- Summarizing system and service resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SvcV-3a can be represented using a SvcV-3a diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.

The SvcV-3a provides a tabular summary of the systemand servicesinteractions specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='']. Each Resource Exchangelisted in the SvcV-3a table should be traceable to Operational Exchange listed in the corresponding [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='']. Each Resource Exchangemay relate to a known Function (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']) that produces or consumes it. DataElements as well as the other type of Resource ExchangeItems are exchanged by Service Providers and Service Requesters represented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='']SV-6 table.

395570066

Each Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>may relate to a known Function (from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) that produces or consumes it. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Data </span>Elements as well as the other type of <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562657 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06383: SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix

- page_id: `249562657`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562657/SvcV-3b+Services-Services+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:38.762+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-3b-services-services-matrix', 'svcv-3b', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396077917

396077919

396077918

**Description**

A SvcV-3b enables a quick overview of all the services resource interactions specified in one or more SvcV-1 Services Context Description models. The SvcV-3b provides a tabular summary of the services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the SvcV-10a Services Rules Model, SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description, and SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description, implemented as orchestrations of services.

The SvcV-3b can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.

The intended usage of the SvcV-3b includes:

- Summarizing service resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SvcV-3b can be represented using a SvcV-3b diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.

This model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10a Services Rules Model' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description' space=''], implemented as orchestrations of services.

396077916

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-3b matrix' space='']

The SvcV-3b provides a tabular summary of the services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the SvcV-10a Services Rules Model, SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description, and SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description, implemented as orchestrations of services.</p><p>The SvcV-3b can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-3b includes:</p><ul><li>Summarizing service resource interactions.</li><li>Interface management.</li><li>Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-3b can be represented using a SvcV-3b diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.</p><p>This model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10a Services Rules Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV- 10a Services Rules Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" /></ac:link>, implemented as orchestrations of services.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2284c6e9-45c1-4164-ab73-42370aceb193"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">396077916</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Exchange" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-3b matrix" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562661 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06384: SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description

- page_id: `249562661`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562661/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:49.386+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-4-services-functionality-description', 'svcv-4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396204750

396204752

396204751

**Description**

The SvcV-4 DoDAF-described Model addresses human and service functionality.

The primary purpose of SvcV-4 is to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the service functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The Services Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of service functions to resources.
- Flow of resources between service functions.

The SvcV-4 is the Services Viewpoint counterpart to the OV-5b Operational Activity Model of the Operational Viewpoint.

The intended usage of the SvcV-4 includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional service requirements.
- Functional decomposition of Services.
- Relate human and service functions.

**Implementation**

SvcV-4 can be represented using:

- SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_4.png']
- SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram which is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_4_flow.png']

The SvcV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''] (in the same way that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] is the behavioral counterpart to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']).

396204748

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function Action' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562671 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06385: SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description BPD

- page_id: `249562671`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562671/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:39.238+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562673 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06386: SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `249562673`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562673/SvcV-5+Services+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:39.331+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-5-operational-activity-to-services-traceability-matrix', 'svcv-5', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-5 addresses the linkage between service functions described in SvcV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree or OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The SvcV-5 depicts the mapping of service functions (and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a service solution. During requirements definition, the SvcV-5 plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SvcV-5 includes:

- Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SvcV-5 can be represented using:

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with SvcV-5 matrix' space=''] describes the mapping between Service s and Operational Activities directly or using the implied relations.

DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term Service Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing—both activities and service functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Service Function is a question of what and how. The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A Service Function specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, the SvcV-5 is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] with the physical specification of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']. Service Functions can be carried out by Resources.

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Is Capable To Perform' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Supports' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-5 addresses the linkage between service functions described in SvcV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree or OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The SvcV-5 depicts the mapping of service functions (and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a service solution. During requirements definition, the SvcV-5 plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-5 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-5 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with SvcV-5 matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">describes the mapping between </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Service</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">s and Operational Activities directly or using the implied relations.</span></li></ul><p>DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term Service Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing—both activities and service functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Service Function is a question of what and how. The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A Service Function specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, the SvcV-5 is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the physical specification of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. Service Functions can be carried out by Resources.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Is Capable To Perform" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Supports" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3e634151-caf8-4f10-a97d-222f92b26569" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562679 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06387: SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `249562679`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562679/SvcV-5+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:39.552+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix** depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create an SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create an SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png" /></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562685 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06388: SvcV-6 Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `249562685`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562685/SvcV-6+Role-based+Services+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:07.920+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix** (SvcV-6) specifies the characteristics of the data exchanged between service roles.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click **+** button.In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Interactions, you can use Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.

##### Generating Reports

The Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix</strong> (SvcV-6) specifies the characteristics of the data exchanged between service roles.</p><h3>Adding existing Service Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Service Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f021b3-2cf1-43b8-8ca7-31a54cac7bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Other cells are read-only.</span></li><li>To create and modify the Service Interactions, you can use Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Generating Reports</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:</span></p><ul><li><span>Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the <strong>Report </strong>button,</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Using a quick report option from the Containment tree</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Through the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="svcv-6_role_based_service_resource_flow_matrix_report.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">An example of the Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix report</span></h6><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562682 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06389: SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `249562682`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562682/SvcV-6+Services+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:49.675+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix** addresses service exchanges between services.

You can add any available kind (such as Information Exchange, Materiel Exchange, Organizational Exchange, Energy Exchange) of Service Exchanges in this table.

##### Adding new Service Exchanges

To add new Service Exchange

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Services Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Service Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Service Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Service Exchanges by clicking the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Service Exchanges are added to the table.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) diagram.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Service Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchange data.

##### Adding Service Functions

To add Service Functions

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Service Functions button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space=''] dialog opens.
3. In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space=''] .
4. Click OK .

##### Generating Reports

The Services Resource Flow Matrix has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Resource Flow Matrix report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562688 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06390: SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix

- page_id: `249562688`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562688/SvcV-7+Services+Measures+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:49.883+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-7-services-measures-matrix', 'svcv-7', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396906475

396906478

396906477

**Description**

The SvcV-7 depicts the measures (metrics) of resources. The Services Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in an SvcV-1 Services Context Description by depicting the characteristics of the resources in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description.

In addition, this model is useful in support of the net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. Service measures for Service Level Agreements for each service and may include a number of service consumers, service usage by consumers, and the minimum, average and maximum response times, allowed downtime, etc. Measures of interest for a Chief Information Office or Program manager may include measures that assess service reuse, process efficiency, and business agility.

The intended usage of the SvcV-7 includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

SvcV-7 can be represented using:

- SvcV-7 Typical measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_7_typical.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='']
- SvcV-7 Actual measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_7_actual.png']
- SvcV-7 actual measures spreadsheet report

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562694 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06391: SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description

- page_id: `249562694`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562694/SvcV-8+Services+Evolution+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:39.786+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-8-services-evolution-description', 'svcv-8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397044898

397044900

397044899

**Description**

The SvcV-8 presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.

The intended usage of the SvcV-8 includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The SvcV-8 view can be represented using a SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

397044897

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Configuration' space='MED']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562705 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06392: SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast

- page_id: `249562705`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562705/SvcV-9+Services+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:40.002+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-9-services-technology-and-skills-forecast', 'svcv-9', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397238836

397238838

397238837

**Description**

The SvcV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description model milestones and linked to Capability Phases.

The SvcV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The SvcV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:

- Emerging capabilities.
- Industry trends.
- Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software services.
- Current and possible future skills.

In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and services, the SvcV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals. In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. As technologies change, like incorporation of Representational State Transfer (REST) services in the Web Services Description Language, this model can present a timeline of technologies related services over time.

The intended usage of the SvcV-9 includes:

- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR Trends Analysis.
- Recruitment Planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.

**Implementation**

SvcV-9 can be represented using a SvcV-9 table.

The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) can be coordinated with architecture transition plans (which the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description' space=''] can support). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading or re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.

If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given architecture, then it may be convenient to combine SvcV-9 with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space=''] into a composite Fit-for-Purpose View.

397238834

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-9 table' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="064d0dc8-cbab-4bf8-9916-f59f20267a05"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397238836</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b69b244-d21e-4e70-9d39-a2894a478e9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397238838</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c32e261c-90e3-4b5a-8bd1-29a9ec885cbc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397238837</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description model milestones and linked to Capability Phases.</p><p>The SvcV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The SvcV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:</p><ul><li>Emerging capabilities.</li><li>Industry trends.</li><li>Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software services.</li><li>Current and possible future skills.</li></ul><p>In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and services, the SvcV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals. In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. As technologies change, like incorporation of Representational State Transfer (REST) services in the Web Services Description Language, this model can present a timeline of technologies related services over time.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-9 includes:</p><ul><li>Forecasting technology readiness against time.</li><li>HR Trends Analysis.</li><li>Recruitment Planning.</li><li>Planning technology insertion.</li><li>Input to options analysis.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-9 can be represented using a SvcV-9 table.</p><p>The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) can be coordinated with architecture transition plans (which the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description" /></ac:link> can support). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading or re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.</p><p>If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given architecture, then it may be convenient to combine SvcV-9 with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ac:link> into a composite Fit-for-Purpose View.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2532cc1e-f9ae-408f-99b9-a4448f990bca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397238834</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Forecast" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-9 table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968240 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06393: Swapping

- page_id: `243968240`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968240/Swapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:54.198+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Refactoring
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Swapping allows counter-change symbols with each other on the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can swap the symbols in the following diagrams:

- In the Activity diagram, you can swap actions, merge or decision nodes, join or fork nodes with each other, if the symbols are connected through control flow.
- In the State diagram, you can swap states or pseudo states (initial state, deep history, join, fork, junction, choice, entry point, etc.) with each other, if symbols are connected through transition.

To swap elements, do one of the following

- Drag one shape onto another. Swap does not work when dragged shape can be added into the target shape (or in other words, can be added as a child).
- On the diagram, select the two shapes that you want to swap, right-click, and from the shortcut menu, select Refactor > Swap .

- Swapping does not work when symbols have different parents.
- If you cannot swap within a Teamwork Cloud project, make sure you have the right to edit the model of this project and then try to lock for edit this diagram.

In the following example, you can see the cases when you can swap the elements and the cases when you cannot swap the elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Rule | Example |
| --- | --- |
| You can swap the elements that are in the same branch | You can swap the following elements:“Select the reading item to remove” with “Confirm deletion”“Request removal confirmation” with “Check if there are loaned copies of reading item”“Check if there are loaned copies of reading item” with “Notify librarian and do not allow removal”“Check if there are loaned copies of reading item” with “Check if reading item is reserved” |
| You cannot swap the elements that have different parents | You cannot swap the following elements:“Select the reading item to remove” with Request removal confirmation, because one element is in the “Librarian” partition and the other is in the “System” partition.“Confirm deletion” with “Check if there are loaned copies of reading item” because one element is in the “Librarian” partition and the other is in the “System” partition. |
| You cannot swap the elements that are in different branches | You cannot swap the following elements:“Notify librarian and do not allow removal” with “Check if reading item is reserved”. |

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562454 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06395: Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives

- page_id: `249562454`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562454/Switching+between+projects+with+different+AF+related+perspectives
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:03:50.799+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A particular behavior is applied when switching between projects with different Architecture Framework (AF) related perspectives.When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a message is displayed.You can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective in that message.

In the following table, see the cases of the actions required when switching between projects A and B.

| Actions of Project A | Project A perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project A | Actions of Project B | Project B perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project B | Required actions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Open Project A | UAF | - | Open project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Open project A | UAF | - | Open project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Create Project A | UAF | MODAF | Create project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Open Project A | UAF | MODAF | Open project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | UAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the UAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to UAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | - | Switch to opened project B | NAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the NAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to NAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | NAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the NAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to NAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2(the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | MODAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the MODAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to MODAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2 (the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |

##### Examples of messages

Here are examples of messages you receive when switching projects with different perspectives.

**Case #1.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Case #2.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']
