<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566437 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06396: Switching to Business Modeling Perspectives

- page_id: `249566437`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566437/Switching+to+Business+Modeling+Perspectives
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:20:29.887+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The business modeling perspectives are dedicated to business process modelers. The perspectives offer a simplified user interface by showing the features that are relevant to business process modeling and hiding the others that are not.

Business Modeler offers two business modeling perspectives. They are as follow:

- Business Analyst perspective that provides set of most often used elements of BPMN standard.
- Business Architect perspective that provides all BPMN elements

To switch to the Business Architect or Business Analyst perspective

1. From the main menu, click Options > Perspectives > Perspectives . The Select Perspectives dialog will open.
2. Select Business Analyst or Business Architect appropriately and click Apply .

**Related resources**

- Customizing and Selecting Perspective

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The business modeling perspectives are dedicated to business process modelers. The perspectives offer a simplified user interface by showing the features that are relevant to business process modeling and hiding the others that are not.</p><p>Business Modeler offers two business modeling perspectives. They are as follow:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c54866ba-8fc0-4767-a825-0c3d5ace4649">Business Analyst perspective that provides set of most often used elements of BPMN standard.</li><li data-uuid="2ddb7eb8-9539-4cad-b58a-3ae5b7896137">Business Architect perspective that provides all BPMN elements</li></ul><p>To switch to the Business Architect or Business Analyst perspective</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="15da3e87-f130-416e-a6a1-3c87bd7edaa3">From the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong>. The <strong>Select Perspectives</strong> dialog will open.</li><li data-uuid="54f3c1a0-599b-4277-b752-778ff7b23389">Select <strong>Business Analyst</strong> or <strong>Business Architect</strong> appropriately and click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related resources</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="fd02191e-687c-4eba-9e22-f95cc713c75c"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Customizing+and+Selecting+Perspective">Customizing and Selecting Perspective</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968293 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06397: Symbol Properties dialog

- page_id: `243968293`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968293/Symbol+Properties+dialog
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-09T18:33:34.521+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, you may define various symbol (or group of symbols) properties, such as fill color, font type, whether to wrap text. The **Symbol Properties** dialog is a non-modal window - you can edit the symbol properties and see the changes on the diagram instantly. In addition, while the dialog is active you can still work with diagrams or other dialogs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Symbol Properties** dialog

1. On the diagram, select a symbol or a group of symbols.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the main menu, select Edit > Symbol > Symbol Properties .
  - From the selected elements' shortcut menu, choose Symbol Properties .
  - Press Alt+Enter.

In the following table, you will find the described common functions that **Symbol Properties** dialog provides.

| Icon | Title | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Apply Style | Button | Click to open the menu with the available . |
|  | Show All Properties | Button | Click to display all the properties for the selected symbol types.Note that the Show All Properties button is displayed only when at least two shapes of different element types are selected, for example, a class and a package. |
|  | Make Default | Button | Click to change the default symbol properties to the properties that are specified in this dialog.WarningIt is highly recommended to use the Make Default button only if you fully understand the symbol styles and how your symbol styles can be affected.WarningThe Make Default button is available in the Teamwork Cloud project only if you have locked the symbol styles for edit. |

If your project is of the earlier version you need to set up a new symbol properties manually.

**Related pages**

- Symbol Properties dialog
- Applying image
- Displaying icon or image
- Symbol styles option group
- Working with Symbol styles
- Properties Inheritance
- Stereotype properties
- Copy and paste symbol style
- Making your own default style for new projects

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968317 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06398: Symbol styles option group

- page_id: `243968317`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968317/Symbol+styles+option+group
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:56.038+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All symbols in the modeling tool are created according to active properties styles. There may be more than one property style in the same project, and the whole style may be applied for the project.

Every style has its own presentation of Diagram, Shape, Path, and Stereotype that you can modify using the **Project Options** dialog in the **Symbol styles** option group. You can set your own options for every model element to the current style.

**Path**, **Shape**, and **Stereotype** branches have the inner structure that helps you find the model element, the representation of which must be changed. The section on the right side of the dialog contains possible choices and instruments to manage them.

The following properties are defined for the formatting symbols:

- Shapes . Set general options for the shapes in the right pane of the Project Options dialog. You can set options for all shapes that appear on the Diagram pane.
- Paths . Set general options for the paths in the right pane of the Project Options dialog. You can set options for all paths that appear on the Diagram pane.
- Diagram . Set general options about a diagram.
- Stereotypes . Set general options for the stereotypes in the right pane of the Project Options dialog. You can set options for all stereotypes that may be applied to elements on the Diagram pane.

##### Changing options for multiple symbols

To change properties for multiple symbols, press Ctrl or Shift and select few symbols in the **Project Options** dialog > **Symbol styles** option group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Shape, path, and diagram options

All shapes, paths and diagrams that can be created in the project, are listed in the **Project Options** dialog. If the Shape, Path, or Diagram branches are selected in the tree, the general properties can be set in the right pane of this dialog.

When expanding any of these option groups, the style for a particular element (diagram) can be created.

To apply a new style to a previously created element symbol

1. In the Project Options dialog , change the element style properties and click the Apply button. The Select Diagrams dialog opens.
2. Select the diagrams to which the element properties will be applied and click OK . The Select Properties to Apply dialog opens.
3. Select the properties to be applied to the element symbol by moving them from the All list to the Selected list.
4. Click **OK** when you are done. If a new style was set, it will be applied for all newly created elements after drawing them on the diagram pane. You can always set the default symbols style to the element by clicking the **Apply Default Symbol Style** button in the main toolbar.

##### Properties extension by diagram

Diagram, shape, path, and stereotype properties can be extended by the particular diagram type. This means that presentation style options will be applied only for the specified element symbol in the specific diagram.

To specify element properties for the selected diagram type

1. In the Project Options dialog, the Symbol Styles option group, do one of the following
  - Selected the desired element and from its shortcut menu, select the diagram type. The diagram is added as an additional branch to the section. [ATTACHMENT filename='extend_by_diagram_1.png']
  - Click the Extend by Diagram button, in the open Extend by Diagram dialog, click the Add Diagram button and select the desired diagram type.
2. Set the style properties for the element. The properties will be applied only in the specified type of diagram.

To remove the extended diagram from the tree, do one of the following

- In the Project Options dialog, select the extended diagram and from the shortcut menu, choose Remove .
- In the Project Options dialog, select the extended diagram and click the Remove button on the right.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967050 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06399: Sync with Excel or CSV files

- page_id: `243967050`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967050/Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:57:35.750+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

43

top

##### Introduction

You can transfer data between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables. After sync, the data on the Excel file of the selected sheet or CSV file matches the data in the modeling tool table. This mechanism is bidirectional and ensures continuous work between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables. If you don't have the ability to collaborate via the internet but you need to share information through Excel or CSV files, you can use this feature. The Excel/CSV file syncing is available in all tables of the modeling tool by using the **Excel/CSV** **Sync**button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Preparing Excel or CSV files to sync

To sync data from Excel or CSV files, the files must meet the following prerequisites:

- The heading is located in the first row.
- When creating relationships and connectors, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contains the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are:
  - the client and supplier of the relationship.
  - the roles of the connector ends A and B.
- When creating connectors, the spreadsheet may optionally contain the owner column. The owner of the connector is determined in the following ways:
  - If the owner property is mapped, the connector is imported to the mapped owner.
  - If there is no owner property, the connector is imported to the element that owns the source and target elements indicated in the file.
    - If no such element exists in the model, the connector is imported to the owner of the element that is indicated as the source in the file.
  - If no qualified owner is found, the element selection dialog opens to select the owner for the connector.

Recommended prerequisites:

- Eliminate merged cells.
- Every column has a unique column name.
- Every row has the same number and name of columns. Every cell has the element name or a qualified element name.

##### How to synchronize your data

To synchronize the table with the Excel or CSV file

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating an empty table' space=''] or open an existing table.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space=''] , select the Element Type or Classifier .
3. Click the **Columns** button and select properties and/or slots to which you want to sync data from the Excel or CSV file.
4. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space=''], click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then choose **Excel/CSV File** > **Select File**. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog, select the Excel or CSV file either from your file system or from the model, if the file is already attached. [How to attach the file to a project >](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attaching%2C+reattaching%2C+and+detaching+files) 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] An alternative way to link Excel or CSV file to the table is to drag it onto the table, hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag), then drop it, and select either**Link Excel/CSV File**or**Attach and Link Excel/CSV File**.You can also use a[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating path variables' space='']to target the needed file, e.g.,*<excelSync>/Requirements.xlsx*.If you want to modify sync options, select on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space=''], click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], then choose **Excel/CSV File** > **Sync Options**and go to step 6. If you want to synchronize data instantly in the ordinal position, go to step 9. - If you select another file, e.g. Vehicle Requirements.xlsx on a modeling tool table that is already linked with the file, e.g. Stakeholder Requirements.xlsx, the previous file named *Stakeholder Requirements.xlsx* is replaced with the latest one *Vehicle Requirements.xlsx*. 
- You can link only one Excel/CSV file with the same modeling tool table.
5. Click Details to expand the dialog.
6. The modeling tool might require refreshing the dialog before setting the mappings. Therefore, if you see this notification at the bottom of the dialog, click **Refresh**. Before uploading the mapping of the older version to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='MCS'], you need to recreate the mapping in the local project and resave it first.
7. In the opened **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog select options. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog' space='']>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you are importing requirements, they are first mapped by ID if the ID column is displayed. If the ID column is not displayed, then the requirements are mapped by name. If the name is changed, then a new requirement is created in the table.
8. Click OK .
9. Import data from the linked file in one of the following ways: Additional optional procedures prior to data import[Using UML properties upon import](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-UsingUMLpropertiesuponimportUsingUMLpropertiesuponimport)[Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-Skippingemptyrowsand/orcolumnsuponimport) - The notification appears on the down-right corner of the modeling tool window (see the following figure), click **Read from File**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
-On the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the **Read From File** command. Read From File exceptionsAfter making any changes in the Excel or CSV file you must save them before using the**Read From File**command. Otherwise, the changes will not be imported to the modeling tool table.Data cannot be imported or imported correctly if:The CSV file format is invalid.Cells are non-editable.Data types of columns are not compatible with source information.Columns do not support information updates.Elements with the same names but different types exist.The owner is not set or updated because this element does not exist in the model.Also, some text formatting may not be transferred.
10. If you synchronize requirements, in the **Select Type** dialog, select the element you want to create in the modeling tool table.
11. Wait while data are imported into the table. The relevant notification displays in the down-right corner, when synchronization is completed. Items synchronized to the SysML v1 requirements table appear in the Model Browser, too. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you made changes in the file and saved them, the table[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space=''] displays automatically and highlights the **New**, **Obsolete**, **Unchanged**, and **Updated** rows. In the figure above, the first requirement is updated, the seventh is newly created, and the second is deleted. All the items in the table are highlighted. The legend above reveals that all new items are highlighted like this. You can change the legend colors or hide the legend from the table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='']>]]>
12. After making changes in the modeling tool table, export that data to the linked file as follows: on the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the **Write To File** command. Additional optional procedures prior to data export[Mapping elements by ID upon export](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-ToexportbyIDMappingelementsbyIDuponexport)[Color marking modified data upon export](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-Colormarkingmodifieddatauponexport) Write To File exceptionsThe data cannot be exported or exported correctly if:The Excel file is corrupted.The CSV file is open.The CSV file is locked or you do not have the necessary permissions to access it. The data is synchronized between the modeling tool table and Excel or CSV file.

]]>

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

After sync, the data on the Excel file of the selected sheet or CSV file matches the data in the modeling tool table. This mechanism is bidirectional and ensures continuous work between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables. If you don't have the ability to collaborate via the internet but you need to share information through Excel or CSV files, you can use this feature. The Excel/CSV file syncing is available in all tables of the modeling tool by using the <strong>Excel/CSV</strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Sync </strong></span>button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png" /></ac:image> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table toolbar.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:queryparams="effects=border-simple,shadow-kn" ac:height="253" ac:width="253"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Excel Linking dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Preparing Excel or CSV files to sync</h3><p>To sync data from Excel or CSV files, the files must meet the following p<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">rerequisites</span>:</p><ul><li>The heading is located in the first row.</li><li>When creating relationships and connectors, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contains the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are:<ul><li>the client and supplier of the relationship.</li><li>the roles of the connector ends A and B.</li></ul></li><li>When creating connectors, the spreadsheet may optionally contain the owner column. The owner of the connector is determined in the following ways:<ul><li>If the owner property is mapped, the connector is imported to the mapped owner. </li><li>If there is no owner property, the connector is imported to the element that owns the source and target elements indicated in the file.<ul><li>If no such element exists in the model, the connector is imported to the owner of the element that is indicated as the source in the file.</li></ul></li><li>If no qualified owner is found, the element selection dialog opens to select the owner for the connector.</li></ul></li></ul><p>Recommended prerequisites:</p><ul><li>Eliminate merged cells.</li><li>Every column has a unique column name.</li><li>Every row has the same number and name of columns.<br />Every cell has the element name or a qualified element name. </li></ul><h3>How to synchronize your data</h3><p>To synchronize the table with the Excel or CSV file</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating an empty table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create new]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or open an existing table.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select the <strong>Element Type</strong> or <strong>Classifier</strong>.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Columns</strong> button and select properties and/or slots to which you want to sync data from the Excel or CSV file.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png" /></ac:image> and then choose <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Select File</strong>. In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select the Excel or CSV file either from your file system or from the model, if the file is already attached. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attaching%2C+reattaching%2C+and+detaching+files" rel="nofollow">How to attach the file to a project &gt;</a><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_file_to_sync.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="757a0bb7-741b-4fdb-a96b-16c06266b303"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">An alternative way to link Excel or CSV file to the table is to drag it onto the table, hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag), then d</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">rop it, and select either </span><strong>Link Excel/CSV File </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">or</span><strong> Attach and Link Excel/CSV File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span></li><li data-uuid="6668bd1a-803a-446d-8a94-3b757721c6e5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>You can also use a<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating path variables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[path variable]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>to target the needed file, e.g.,<span> </span><em>&lt;excelSync&gt;/Requirements.xlsx</em>.</span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you want to modify sync options, select on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png" /></ac:image>, then choose <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Sync Options </strong>and go to step 6. If you want to synchronize data instantly in the ordinal position, go to step 9.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ddb2eed-5c4a-4d08-9d70-79b037b2629a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>- If you select another file, e.g. Vehicle Requirements.xlsx on a modeling tool table that is already linked with the file, e.g. Stakeholder Requirements.xlsx, the previous file named <em>Stakeholder Requirements.xlsx</em> is replaced with the latest one <em>Vehicle Requirements.xlsx</em>.<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">- You can link only one Excel/CSV file with the same modeling tool table.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>Details</strong> to expand the dialog.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The modeling tool might require refreshing the dialog before setting the mappings. Therefore, if you see this notification at the bottom of the dialog, click <strong>Refresh</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc32914e-2e0f-4a7f-a554-54b87d7b0e4c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Before uploading the mapping of the older version to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you need to recreate the mapping in the local project and resave it first.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the opened <strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog select options. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about sync options >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_sync_options_v2.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01a061d7-847d-4e1e-8e2a-7e50afb0709f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you are importing requirements, they are first mapped by ID if the ID column is displayed. If the ID column is not displayed, then the requirements are mapped by name. If the name is changed, then a new requirement is created in the table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Import data from the linked file in one of the following ways:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="231f1b0a-d1c5-4a3f-a25b-2598fb14d149"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional optional procedures prior to data import</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-UsingUMLpropertiesuponimportUsingUMLpropertiesuponimport">Using UML properties upon import</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-Skippingemptyrowsand/orcolumnsuponimport">Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">- The notification appears on the down-right corner of the modeling tool window (see the following figure), click <strong>Read from File</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:height="98"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_sync_options_notification.png" /></ac:image><br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">- </span>On the table toolbar, click  <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png" /></ac:image> and s</span>elect the <strong>Read From File</strong> command.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5700a63e-4f11-460c-8536-10ac29aa9221"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Read From File exceptions </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After making any changes in the Excel or CSV file you must save them before using the </span><strong>Read From File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> command. Otherwise, the changes will not be imported to the modeling tool table.</span></p><p>Data cannot be imported or imported correctly if:</p><ul><li>The CSV file format is invalid.</li><li>Cells are non-editable.</li><li>Data types of columns are not compatible with source information.</li><li>Columns do not support information updates.</li><li>Elements with the same names but different types exist.</li><li>The owner is not set or updated because this element does not exist in the model.</li></ul><p>Also, some text formatting may not be transferred.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you synchronize requirements, in the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, select the element you want to create in the modeling tool table.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Wait while data are imported into the table. The relevant notification displays in the down-right corner, when synchronization is completed. Items synchronized to the SysML v1 requirements table appear in the Model Browser, too.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="read_from_excel_after_changes_example.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3eb5372d-a14b-4270-806b-2c3b50ed84f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you made changes in the file and saved them, the table<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Legend]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> displays automatically and highlights the <strong>New</strong>, <strong>Obsolete</strong>, <strong>Unchanged</strong>, and <strong>Updated</strong> rows. In the figure above, the first requirement is updated, the seventh is newly created, and the second is deleted. All the items in the table are highlighted. The legend above reveals that all new items are highlighted like this. You can change the legend colors or hide the legend from the table. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about legends >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">After making changes in the modeling tool table, export that data to the linked file as follows: on the table toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png" /></ac:image> and s</span>elect the <strong>Write To File</strong> command.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c8f55ff-c82a-422e-a84a-9580401b1389"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional optional procedures prior to data export</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-ToexportbyIDMappingelementsbyIDuponexport">Mapping elements by ID upon export</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-Colormarkingmodifieddatauponexport">Color marking modified data upon export</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11c7a2a7-93bc-45c9-8d6b-482ec303e615"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Write To File exceptions </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The data cannot be exported or exported correctly if:</p><ul><li>The Excel file is corrupted.</li><li><p>The CSV file is open.</p></li><li><p>The CSV file is locked or you do not have the necessary permissions to access it.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The data is synchronized between the modeling tool table and Excel or CSV file.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ac941ff6-9670-4e8f-8329-c9d42b30b272"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Tables" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing data from Excel and CSV files" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968496 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06400: Synchronization dialog environment

- page_id: `243968496`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968496/Synchronization+dialog+environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:00.488+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Parameters synchronization
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Synchronization** dialog represents those elements whose parameters are not synchronized and provides the ability to restore the synchronization.

In this section, you will find brief information for opening the **Synchronization** dialog and for each button that can be useful while working in the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the Synchronization dialog, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, right-click the invalid element. On the shortcut menu, point to Validation , then to the validation group, and click the Synchronize Manually command.
- On the diagram pane, select the invalid shape. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='validation_invalid.png'] button, and then click the Synchronize Manually command.
- In the Validation Results window, click the Solve button and then click the Synchronize Manually command. For more information on how to open the Validation Results window, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space=''] .

###### The source and target elements area

The following table describes the buttons in the source and target areas of the Synchronization dialog.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Source elements area: |  |
| Up | Click to shift the selected source element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements. |
| Down | Click to shift the selected source element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements. |
| Remove | Click to remove the selected source element from the project. |
| Edit | Click to open the Specification window of the selected source element. |
| Target elements area: |  |
| Up | Click to shift the selected target element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements. |
| Down | Click to shift the selected target element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements. |
| Remove | Click to remove the selected target element from the project. |
| Edit | Click to open the Specification window of the selected target element. |

###### Buttons in the Synchronization dialog

The following table describes the Synchronization dialog buttons.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Synchronize All | Click the button to synchronize all the not synchronized elements. Then from the menu select the straightforward or backward synchronization. For example, when synchronizing Parameters (Activity) and Pins (Call Behavior Action) you can select to synchronize one of the following:Pins (Call Behavior Action) by Parameters (Activity)Parameters (Activity) by Pins (Call Behavior Action) |
| Synchronize Selected | Click to synchronize the selected parameter. Note that the button is disabled if no parameters are selected in the list.After you click the Synchronize Selected button, the following menu appears:Create -> - Click to create the target element with the same properties as the source element.<-Create - Click to create the source element to be created with the same properties as the target element.Update -> - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the target element according to the properties of the source element.<-Update - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the source element according to the properties of the target element.Note that the Update command exists only if there are properties to update. |

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Parameters synchronization' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='']

On the shortcut menu, point to <strong>Validation</strong>, then to the validation group, and click the <strong>Synchronize Manually</strong> command.</li><li>On the diagram pane, select the invalid shape. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_invalid.png" /></ac:image> button, and then click the <strong>Synchronize Manually</strong> command.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation Results</strong> window, click the <strong>Solve</strong> button and then click the <strong>Synchronize Manually</strong> command. For more information on how to open the <strong>Validation Results</strong> window, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><h4>The source and target elements area</h4><p>The following table describes the buttons in the source and target areas of the Synchronization dialog.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><strong>Source elements area:</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Up</td><td><p>Click to shift the selected source element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Down</td><td><p>Click to shift the selected source element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Remove</td><td><p>Click to remove the selected source element from the project.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Edit</td><td><p>Click to open the Specification window of the selected source element.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><strong>Target elements area:</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Up</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to shift the selected target element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Down</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to shift the selected target element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Remove</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to remove the selected target element from the project.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Edit</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to open the Specification window of the selected target element.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h4>Buttons in the Synchronization dialog</h4><p>The following table describes the Synchronization dialog buttons.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Synchronize All</strong></p></td><td><p>Click the button to synchronize all the not synchronized elements. Then from the menu select the straightforward or backward synchronization. For example, when synchronizing Parameters (Activity) and Pins (Call Behavior Action) you can select to synchronize one of the following:</p><ul><li>Pins (Call Behavior Action) by Parameters (Activity)</li><li>Parameters (Activity) by Pins (Call Behavior Action)</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Synchronize Selected</strong></p></td><td><p>Click to synchronize the selected parameter. Note that the button is disabled if no parameters are selected in the list.</p><p>After you click the Synchronize Selected button, the following menu appears:<br /><strong>Create -&gt;</strong> - <strong>Click</strong> to create the target element with the same properties as the source element.<br /><strong>&lt;-Create</strong> - Click to create the source element to be created with the same properties as the target element.<br /><strong>Update -&gt;</strong> - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the target element according to the properties of the source element.<br /><strong>&lt;-Update</strong> - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the source element according to the properties of the target element.</p><p>Note that the Update command exists only if there are properties to update.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9a36f92-37a4-4db1-a58d-8aea935ced28"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="640cb8fa-31dd-4323-99bc-fa42a94cd8a6" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44ba6805-c2bb-45ad-83b9-cbc485ec676b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Parameters synchronization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with model elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971121 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06401: Syntax rules

- page_id: `243971121`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971121/Syntax+rules
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:24.042+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Generating reports from the command line
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1333700293

1333700294

1333700293

- Command parameters containing spaces must be enclosed in quotes, for example, "UML Standard Profile".
- Command parameters are case sensitive, except project, imageFormat and recursive which are not case-sensitive.
- Blank spaces (“ “) separate commands and parameters.
- To specify a package name that contains a semicolon (;), you can use a backslash (\). For example, the package name “com;nomagic” can be typed by using “com\;nomagic”. The backslash followed by a semicolon (\;) is not considered to be a character separator.
- For a parameter that contains unicode characters such as Thai, you can use the xml properties file to generate a report. The properties file does not support the unicode encoding.
- A field entry consists of a name-value pair. The name and value formats are specified by [name=value] pattern strings. A valid name must start with a letter (a-z or A-Z) and can be followed by any letter, digit, or underscore.
- You can specify a field name that consists of brackets ( [ ] ) by using backslashes ( \ ). For example, "[author=NoMagic][Revision=[1.0]]" will be typed as "[author=NoMagic][Revision=\[1.0\]]".
- Either -package or -element can be used with the Generate command. For example, you can use -package without -element.
- If -upload is specified to the command line. Its value is validated and executed after a report has been generated.

1333700292

**Related pages**

- Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server
- Properties filename
- Uploading generated reports to servers
- Using the Generate command to generate reports

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d06120f2-d1d0-46b9-8f81-96ee9d547880"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1333700293</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="674e4a9e-a81a-4d6f-ac36-5e13ef4b2376"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1333700294</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a68051a-9f3c-4340-a13d-83f96c484185"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1333700293</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Command parameters containing spaces must be enclosed in quotes, for example, &quot;UML Standard Profile&quot;.</li><li>Command parameters are case sensitive, except project, imageFormat and recursive which are not case-sensitive.</li><li>Blank spaces (“ “) separate commands and parameters.</li><li>To specify a package name that contains a semicolon (;), you can use a backslash (\). For example, the package name “com;nomagic” can be typed by using “com\;nomagic”. The backslash followed by a semicolon (\;) is not considered to be a character separator.</li><li>For a parameter that contains unicode characters such as Thai, you can use the xml properties file to generate a report. The properties file does not support the unicode encoding.</li><li>A field entry consists of a name-value pair. The name and value formats are specified by [name=value] pattern strings. A valid name must start with a letter (a-z or A-Z) and can be followed by any letter, digit, or underscore.</li><li>You can specify a field name that consists of brackets ( [ ] ) by using backslashes ( \ ). For example, &quot;[author=NoMagic][Revision=[1.0]]&quot; will be typed as &quot;[author=NoMagic][Revision=\[1.0\]]&quot;.</li><li>Either -package or -element can be used with the Generate command. For example, you can use -package without -element.</li><li>If -upload is specified to the command line. Its value is validated and executed after a report has been generated.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10aeaa6d-401e-4206-bd71-a93468bbd0c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1333700292</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server">Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Properties+filename">Properties filename</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Uploading+generated+reports+to+servers">Uploading generated reports to servers</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Using+the+Generate+command+to+generate+reports">Using the Generate command to generate reports</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247038083 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06402: SysML 1.7 support

- page_id: `247038083`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/247038083/SysML+1.7+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-05T13:49:39.522+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

As of version 2022x, the SysML Plugin supports the SysML 1.7 specification.

##### QUDV model library for owned operations

The*dependsOnUnits*owned operation has been redefined with the*dependsOnUnits*operation owned by*Unit*for the following blocks:*SimpleUnit*,*DerivedUnit*, and *ConversionBasedUnit.*The *dependsOnQuantityKinds*owned operation for the *SimpleQuanitityKind* block has been redefined with the*dependsOnQuantityKinds*operation owned by *QuantityKind*.

##### *Prefix::Symbol* in the QUDV model library

In the QUDV model library, the multiplicity of*Prefix::Symbol*has been changed from [1] to [0..1].

##### Renamed allocation compartments

The **Apply SysML 1.7 Allocation Compartment Naming** symbol property allows renaming allocation compartments according to the SysML 1.7 standard. The "allocatedTo" compartment is renamed to "allocatedToElements", and the "allocatedFrom" compartment to"allocatedElements".

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Renaming allocation compartments according to the SysML 1.7 standard.

To rename allocation compartments for an element shape

1. Right-click an element shape and select Symbol Properties .
2. In the open dialog, set the Apply SysML 1.7 Allocation Compartment Naming property to true .

To rename allocation compartments for all new shapes of a specific element type

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, go to Options > Project .
2. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, expand the Symbol styles property group.
3. Find and select the desired element type.
4. On the right side of the Project Options dialog, set the Apply SysML 1.7 Allocation Compartment Naming property to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">As of version 2022x, the SysML Plugin supports the SysML 1.7 specification.</span></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br />QUDV model library for owned operations</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The<span> </span><em>dependsOnUnits</em><span> </span>owned operation has been redefined with the<span> </span><em>dependsOnUnits </em>operation owned by<span> </span><em>Unit</em><span> </span>for the following blocks:<span> </span><em>SimpleUnit</em>,<span> </span><em>DerivedUnit</em>, and <em>ConversionBasedUnit.</em><span> </span>The <em>dependsOnQuantityKinds </em>owned operation for the <em>SimpleQuanitityKind</em> block has been redefined with the<span> </span><em>dependsOnQuantityKinds</em><span> </span>operation owned by <em>QuantityKind</em>.</span></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><em>Prefix::Symbol</em> in the QUDV model library</span></h3><p>In the QUDV model library, the multiplicity of<span> </span><em>Prefix::Symbol</em><span> </span>has been changed from <span class="error">[1]</span> to <span class="error">[0..1]</span>.</p><h3><br />Renamed allocation compartments</h3><p>The <strong>Apply SysML 1.7 Allocation Compartment Naming</strong> symbol property allows renaming allocation compartments according to the SysML 1.7 standard. The &quot;allocatedTo&quot; compartment is renamed to &quot;allocatedToElements&quot;, and the &quot;allocatedFrom&quot; compartment to&quot;allocatedElements&quot;.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="renaming_allocation_compartments.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Renaming allocation compartments according to the SysML 1.7 standard.</h6><p><br /></p><p>To rename allocation compartments for an element shape</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="0d6c2567-cd91-4373-abf0-4539a6956e6a">Right-click an element shape and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="2bbce0b9-2bbf-4256-a6b9-489b5915021e">In the open dialog, set the <strong>Apply SysML 1.7 Allocation Compartment Naming</strong> property to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To rename allocation compartments for all new shapes of a specific element type</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="06624635-4abd-481f-a233-ae12dcb36ec9">In the main menu of a modeling tool, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="f1785ccb-2b37-4461-ba25-85edc53c45b7">On the left side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> property group.</li><li data-uuid="6a801479-8852-4a0c-9876-dcef76e00b64">Find and select the desired element type.</li><li data-uuid="dd033220-6598-46a7-8ce4-69948c086c26">On the right side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Apply SysML 1.7 Allocation Compartment Naming</strong> property to <em>true</em>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561853 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06403: SysML Activity Diagram

- page_id: `249561853`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561853/SysML+Activity+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:11.337+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > SysML v1 diagram descriptions
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

949321384

949321396

949321386

Activity diagram describes control, input, and output flows among actions. It represents the system business and operational work flows. It captures actions and display their results. It is typically used for business process modeling and used in situations where all or most of the events represent the completion of internally generated actions.

Though Activity diagrams are often classified alongside interaction diagrams, they actually focus on the flows driven by internal processes (as opposed to external events).

SysML extends control in Activity diagrams and provides extensions that might be very loosely grouped under the term “continuous”, but are generally applicable to any distributed flow of information and physical items through a system. It also introduces probability concepts to activities.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

949321383

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accept Change Structural Feature Event Action' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Change Structural Feature Event' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Invocation on Nested Port Action' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Trigger on Nested Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocation Mode' space='']

It represents the system business and operational work flows. It captures actions and display their results. It is typically used for business process modeling and used in situations where all or most of the events represent the completion of internally generated actions.</p><p>Though Activity diagrams are often classified alongside interaction diagrams, they actually focus on the flows driven by internal processes (as opposed to external events).</p><p>SysML extends control in Activity diagrams and provides extensions that might be very loosely grouped under the term “continuous”, but are generally applicable to any distributed flow of information and physical items through a system. It also introduces probability concepts to activities.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Activity Diagram" ac:alt="Activity Diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="activity_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb822155-9699-4a06-89a1-0d39552d813f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">949321383</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Accept Change Structural Feature Event Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Change Structural Feature Event" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Invocation on Nested Port Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Trigger on Nested Port" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocation Mode" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561876 space=MT version=6 -->
## PAGE 06404: SysML Allocation Matrix

- page_id: `249561876`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561876/SysML+Allocation+Matrix
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-08T16:37:32.548+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > SysML v1 diagram descriptions
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SysML Allocation matrix enables you to analyze, create, and modify Allocate relationships. Rows in the matrix represent an element that can be the client element of the Allocate relationship. Columns in the matrix represent elements that can be the supplier elements of the Allocate relationship. You can set your own element types that you want to allocate in the criteria area or you can create a matrix with predefined element types in the criteria area by selecting one of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocation Mode' space='']

The example below is created by using the[distiller model.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9919520/distiller%20model.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1505483436734&api=v2)sample model that comes only with[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v1 modeling' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SysML Allocation Matrix.

#### INFO: How to work with SysML Allocation matrices

How to work with SysML Allocation matrices

The SysML Allocation matrices are based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='']. Thus, all its procedures are allowable for SysML Allocation matrices:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocation Mode' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can set your own element types that you want to allocate in the criteria area or  you can create a matrix with predefined element types in the criteria area by selecting one of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocation Mode" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Allocation Matrix mode.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The example below is created by using the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9919520/distiller%20model.mdzip?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1505483436734&amp;api=v2">distiller model.mdzip</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> sample model that comes only with </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML v1 modeling" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v1 Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="allocation_matrix.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">SysML Allocation Matrix.<br /></span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ef6d205-79ee-4dba-a735-2651c6ab3f41"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to work with SysML Allocation matrices</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The SysML Allocation matrices</span> are based on <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency Matrix" /></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561837 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06405: SysML Block Definition Diagram

- page_id: `249561837`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561837/SysML+Block+Definition+Diagram
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-10-16T12:20:59.943+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > SysML v1 diagram descriptions
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Block Definition Diagram defines the features of a block and any relationships between blocks such as associations, generalizations, and dependencies, in terms of properties, operations, and relationships (for example, a system hierarchy or a system classification tree).

Block Definition Diagrams are based on UML class diagrams and include restrictions and extensions as defined by SysML v1. They are generally used to display systems of blocks or show a system dictionary and/or extensions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Domain' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='External' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='System' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subsystem in SysML v1' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='System Context' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Specification' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Type' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quantity Kind' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Units' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561793 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06406: SysML Callout Style

- page_id: `249561793`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561793/SysML+Callout+Style
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-08T16:53:25.098+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Working with model elements in SysML v1
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML Callout Style is applied for tagged values by default in Compartment area of the:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED']Part Property
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MED']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To return the non-standard style

1. Open Symbol Properties dialog.
2. Set the Apply SysML Callout Style property value to false.

To hide the element type before its name

1. Open Symbol Properties dialog.
2. Set the Show SysML Element Type property value to false .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SysML Callout Style is applied for tagged values by default in Compartment area of the:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Requirement" /></ac:link></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Part</span> Property</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Note" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Note ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="SysML Callout style" ac:alt="SysML Callout style"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sysml_callout_style.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To return the non-standard style</p><hr /><ol><li>Open <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog.</li><li>Set the<strong> Apply SysML Callout Style</strong> property value to <em>false.</em></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To hide the element type before its name</p><hr /><ol><li>Open <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show SysML Element Type </strong>property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="SysML Callout Style property" ac:alt="SysML Callout Style property"><ri:attachment ri:filename="callout_style_property.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565592 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06407: SysML Compliance mode changes

- page_id: `249565592`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565592/SysML+Compliance+mode+changes
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:01:00.775+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML v1. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML v1 elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.

The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype and SysML PartProperty stereotype):

- Capability Property
- Concept Role
- Known Resource
- Operational Role
- Problem Domain
- Resource Role
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML v1. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML v1 elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.</p><p>The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype  and SysML PartProperty stereotype):</p><ul><li>Capability Property</li><li>Concept Role</li><li>Known Resource</li><li>Operational Role</li><li>Problem Domain</li><li>Resource Role</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li></ul>
````

- page_id: `249565555`

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565555/SysML+Compliance+mode+changes+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 2

- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML v1. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML v1 elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.

The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype and SysML PartProperty stereotype):

- Capability Property
- Concept Role
- Known Resource
- Operational Role
- Problem Domain
- Resource Role
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML v1. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML v1 elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.</p><p>The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype  and SysML PartProperty stereotype):</p><ul><li>Capability Property</li><li>Concept Role</li><li>Known Resource</li><li>Operational Role</li><li>Problem Domain</li><li>Resource Role</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561840 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06409: SysML Internal Block Diagram

- page_id: `249561840`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561840/SysML+Internal+Block+Diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:26:37.923+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > SysML v1 diagram descriptions
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Internal Block Diagram is based on UML composite structure diagram and includes restrictions and extensions as defined by SysML v1. An Internal Block Diagram captures the internal structure of a Block in terms of properties and connections among properties. A Block includes properties so that its values, parts, and references to other blocks can be specified. However, whereas an Internal Block Diagram created for a Block (as an inner element) will only display the inner elements of a classifier (parts, ports, and connectors), an Internal Block Diagram created for a package will display additional elements (shapes, notes, and comments).

All properties and connectors that appear inside an Internal Block Diagram belong to (are owned by) a Block whose name is written in the diagram heading. That particular Block is the context of the diagram. SysML v1 allows any property (part) to be shown in an Internal Block Diagram to display compartments within the property (or part) symbol.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561235 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06410: SysML Internal Block Diagram context

- page_id: `249561235`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561235/SysML+Internal+Block+Diagram+context
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:01.307+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Decomposing Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']SysML Internal Block Diagram for an element which cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='']), the new context element (which is a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']) is created automatically.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure Diagram context' space='']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="ancestor-link parent-link">SysML Internal Block Diagram</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> for an element which cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link>), the new context element (which is a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link>) is created automatically.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b4be08f-61f2-4e64-8276-66f137cc9f6c"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Composite Structure Diagram context" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561843 space=MT version=7 -->
## PAGE 06411: SysML Package Diagram

- page_id: `249561843`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561843/SysML+Package+Diagram
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-09-08T16:32:21.632+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > SysML v1 diagram descriptions
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Package diagram typically enables you to organize models by partitioning model elements into packageable elements and establishing dependencies between packages and/or model elements within these packages. Since Package diagram is used to organize models in packages and views, it can include a wide array of packageable elements.

A package is a construct that enables you to organize model elements, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'], into groups. Packages define namespaces for packageable elements. Model elements from one package can be imported and/or accessed by another package. This organizational principle is intended to help establish unique naming of the model elements and avoid overloading a particular model element's name. Packages can also be shown on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement Diagram' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package import' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with packages' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with dependencies' space='MED']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Packages define namespaces for packageable elements. Model elements from one package can be imported and/or accessed by another package. This organizational principle is intended to help establish unique naming of the model elements and avoid overloading a particular model element's name. Packages can also be shown on <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Block Definition diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Requirement Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Requirement diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="abf1775b-421e-40d7-9427-cc7de7190fa3"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d880adf8-1675-4afb-a89e-b83614c63879"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Model" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="35a62ed4-f623-4f5b-81d9-b7ba3ccbc4b5"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Package import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="211f63a1-5e93-45d3-8e18-4cc3dc0cb9a6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="792a5b35-6b57-482f-ae4e-3bfe45591d52"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Working with packages" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="e7bfe791-bea3-45f7-91af-bb84a2cd94fa"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Working with dependencies" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561846 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06412: SysML Parametric Diagram

- page_id: `249561846`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561846/SysML+Parametric+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:11.125+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > SysML v1 diagram descriptions
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

36056666

36056669

36056667

SysML Parametric diagramParametric diagram

Parametric diagram is designed to describe mathematical equations by modeling elements, e.g. to count breaking distance of a vehicle.

Parametric diagram can be defined as restricted forms of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']. They are similar to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']Internal Block diagrams except that the only connectors allowed are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding Connector' space='MED'], each having at least one end connected to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED']Constraint Parameter.

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Block. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

Constrained Properties typically have simple value types that can also carry units, quantity kinds, and probability distributions. This allows for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Property' space='MED'] that may be deeply nested within a containing hierarchy to be referenced at the outer containing level. The context for the usages of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] must also be specified in a parametric diagram to maintain the proper namespaces for the nested properties.

The state of the system can be specified in terms of the values of some of its properties. A change in state will result in a different set of constraint equations to be recalculated. This can be accommodated by specifying constraints that are conditioned on the value of the property with state. Parametric diagrams can be used to support trade-off analysis. A Constraint Block can define an objective function to compare alternative solutions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

36056664

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enforce Ports Compatibility mode' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property path notation' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Moe' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Objective Function' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding Connector' space='MED']

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. ### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562044 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06416: SysML supported and unsupported concepts

- page_id: `249562044`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562044/SysML+supported+and+unsupported+concepts
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:57:22.211+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Simulink export
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1401802970

1401802981

1401802959

**On this page**

1401802980

##### **Supported SysML v1 concepts**

The main (selected root owning Block) is exported into a separate *.slx file with a Block name.

All other used or referenced Blocks are exported as one library *.slx file.

- Blocks which have classifier behavior as statemachine are exported as Stateflow blocks.
- Blocks which behavior is described by Parametric equations are exported as SFunctions or Simscape models.
- If blocks have bidirectional (inout) ports, Simscape should be selected as export option, as Simulink only supports unidirectional inports and outports.
- Both flow ports and proxy ports are supported if they represent one primitive flow type (typed by Real, Integer, Boolean, or one of their specializations).
- Connector must link ports, can’t be attached directly to the part.
- Ports must be typed.
- Proxy port/Interface block with more than 1 flow property (composite signals or buses)
- InstanceSpecifications can be used to define or overwrite initial parameter values.

##### **Not supported SysML v1 concepts**

- Behaviors defined as Activity diagrams or Interactions (Sequence diagrams)
- Mixing of Parametrics and StateMachine in one Block
- Enumerations
- Multiplicities

More constraints and examples can be found in SysPhS specification document. Click [here](https://www.omg.org/spec/SysPhS/1.0/PDF) to access it.

**Diagram layout**

Shape and path positions in IBD and Statemachine diagrams are exported, however result is not exactly the same sometimes and can be changed or distorted on the Simulink side.

Note that Simulink only allows input ports on the left, outputs on the right, so adjust your SysML IBD diagrams accordingly to get best visual result and avoid automatic ports and connectors replacements.

- First owned IBD is exported as Simulink block diagram in case of multiple owned IBDs in a Block.
- Nested symbols such as nested parts or ports are not supported
- If there is more than one connector going in or out of same port, Simulink will merge them into one and adds the branching point.
