<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 12.0941%;" /><col style="width: 87.9059%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1">UAF 1.2</th><th colspan="1">Description</th></tr><tr><td>Actual Effect</td><td>A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Outcome</td><td>Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an &quot;end effect&quot;.</td></tr><tr><td>Capability Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Challenge</td><td>An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Challenge Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Compares To</td><td>An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result.</td></tr><tr><td>Creates</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset.</td></tr><tr><td>Driver</td><td>A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise.</td></tr><tr><td>Driver Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Effect</td><td>A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes.</td></tr><tr><td>Enables</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective.</td></tr><tr><td>Enterprise Mission</td><td>Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision.</td></tr><tr><td>Enterprise Objective</td><td>A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Evoked By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity.</td></tr><tr><td>Governed By</td><td>An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs.</td></tr><tr><td>Impacted By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity.</td></tr><tr><td>Maps To Goal</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective.</td></tr><tr><td>Motivated By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way.</td></tr><tr><td>Operational Asset</td><td>An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Operational Information allowing them to own Operational Information Role.</td></tr><tr><td>Opportunity</td><td>An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges.</td></tr><tr><td>Owns Value</td><td>An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item.</td></tr><tr><td>Phases</td><td>An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. </td></tr><tr><td>Presented By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver.</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Asset</td><td>An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Resource Information allowing them to own Resource Information Roles</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Information Role*</td><td>A usage of Resource Information that exists in the context of an Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Resource Information elements.</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Service</td><td>A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services.</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Service Interface</td><td>A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize.</td></tr><tr><td>Security Risk</td><td>The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65].</td></tr><tr><td>Sequence</td><td>A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Architecture</td><td>An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Contract</td><td>A constraint governing the use of one or more Services.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Control Flow</td><td>An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange</td><td>Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange Item</td><td>An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange Kind</td><td><p>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange. The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:</p><ul><li>Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.</li><li>Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Service Object Flow</td><td>An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal</td><td>A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal Property</td><td>A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal.</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Constraint</td><td>A Rule governing a Capability.</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Exchange</td><td>Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Information</td><td>Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Item</td><td>An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Item Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Stream</td><td>An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020].</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">* For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565486 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06486: UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Renamed and removed elements

- page_id: `249565486`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565486/UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+Renamed+and+removed+elements
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:59:55.887+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element UAF 1.0 [name] | Element UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Achieved Effect | Achieves |
| Capability Property | Capability Role |
| Consumes* | Supports |
| Data Element | Resource Information |
| Data Model | Information Model |
| Data Model Kind | Information Model Kind |
| Data Role | Resource Information Role |
| Desired Effect | Desires |
| Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |
| Information Element | Operational Information |
| Information Role | Operational Information Role |
| Security Property* | Operational Information Role |
| Service Specification | Service |
| Service Specification Role | Service Role |
| Organization In Enterprise | Organization In Phase |

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element | Property UAF 1.0 [name] | Property UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |
| OperationalExchange | trustlevel | trustLevel |
| ResponsibleFor | ResponsibleRoleKind | responsibleRoleKind |
| Architecture | desribedBy | describedBy |
| ArchitecturalDescription | viewPoint | viewpoint |

**Removed properties:**

- Concern::enterprisePhase
- Condition::conditionKind
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: resourceArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: operationalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern
- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect
- DesiredEffect::achievedEffect
- EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase
- EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase
- Risk::riskOwner

**Removed elements:**

- Enduring Task*
- Capability For Task

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 06487: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Changes in diagrams, tables, and matrices

- page_id: `249565478`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565478/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Changes+in+diagrams+tables+and+matrices
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:55:05.874+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

New diagrams, tables, and matrices:

- Architecture Management diagram
- Security Controls diagram
- Security Constraints table
- Services Role-based Connectivity Table
- Services Connectivity Table
- Services Connectivity Matrix
- Strategic Constraint table
- Strategic Information diagram
- Strategic Motivation diagram
- Strategic Processes
- Functions to Operational Activities Matrix
- Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Project Mapping Matrix

The following table shows how the tables, diagrams, and matrices were renamed consistent with the UAF 1.2 support.

| Name in UAF 1.1 | Name in UAF 1.2 |
| --- | --- |
| Capabilities to Actual Enduring Tasks Mapping Matrix | Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping Matrix |
| Service Specifications to Capabilities Mapping matrix | Services to Capabilities Mapping matrix |
| Operational Activities to Service Specifications Mapping matrix | Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix |
| Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix | Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix |
| Operational Interaction Scenarios | Operational Sequences |
| Services Interaction Scenarios | Services Sequences |
| Personnel Interaction Scenarios | Personnel Sequences |
| Resources Interaction Scenarios | Resources Sequences |
| Operational Interaction Scenarios BPD | Operational Sequences BPD |
| Personnel Interaction Scenarios BPD | Personnel Sequences BPD |
| Resources Interaction Scenarios BPD | Resources Sequences BPD |
| Conceptual Data Model | Strategic Information |
| Logical Data Model | Operational Information |
| Physical Data Model | Resources Information |
| Actual Enterprise Phases Gantt Chart | Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart |

Updated diagrams, tables, and matrices:

| Name | Note |
| --- | --- |
| SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix | In UAF 1.2, Associations were changed to Service Exchanges |
| Implementation matrix | The Implements relationship is available between the following client and supplier pairs:Resource Performer and Operational AgentFunction and Operational Activity or Service FunctionResource Interface and Service Interface or Operational InterfaceResource Connector and Operational Connector or Resource ConnectorResource Exchange and Operational ExchangeResource Role and Operational RoleResource Information and Operational informationArchitecture or Operational Activity and Actual Strategic PhaseResource Service and ServiceOperational Information and Strategic Information |
| SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills ForecastSvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills ForecastStdV-2 Standards ForecastNSV-9 Technology ForecastNTV-2 Technical Standards ForecastSV-9 Technology & Skills ForecastTV-2 Standards ForecastP1 Resource Types Technology ForecastA8 Standards ForecastStandards RoadmapResources ForecastPersonnel Forecast | It is possible to add the Actual Strategic Phase as a column in the table.In the 3rd step of the wizard, you can select Actual Strategic Phases. |
| Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping MatrixLr Responsibility MatrixAcV-1 Responsibility MatrixPV-1 Responsibility MatrixNPV-1 Responsibility Matrix | The row scope was changed from Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Responsible Resources. |
| Actual Resources Taxonomy Matrix | The row scope was changed from Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Responsible Resources. |
| Resources to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| Operational Activity to Services Traceability | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| UPDM Capability Behavioral Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Capability Structural Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Implementation Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Operational Activity Decomposition Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Operational Activity Implementation Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Organization Structure Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Resource Performer Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">NPV-1 Responsibility Matrix</span></li></ul></td><td><ul><li>The row scope was changed from Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Responsible Resources.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Actual Resources Taxonomy Matrix</span></td><td><ul><li>The row scope was changed from Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Responsible Resources.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Resources to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Operational Activity to Services Traceability</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UPDM Capability Behavioral Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UPDM Capability Structural Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UPDM Implementation Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UPDM Operational Activity Decomposition Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UPDM Operational Activity Implementation Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UPDM Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UPDM Organization Structure Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UPDM Resource Performer Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565461 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06488: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Changes of element metaclass and type

- page_id: `249565461`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565461/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Changes+of+element+metaclass+and+type
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:46:59.965+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.

| Element | UAF 1.1 [Metaclass] | UAF 1.2 [Metaclass] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| WholeLifeEnterprise | Class | InstanceSpecification |

###### Changes types of Elements

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.

| Element/Property | UAF 1.1 [Type] | UAF 1.2 [Type] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| EnterpriseGoal::benefit | String | ValueItem |

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="1"><p>Element</p></th><th><p>UAF 1.1 [Metaclass]</p></th><th><p><span>UAF 1</span>.2 [Metaclass]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">WholeLifeEnterprise</span></td><td colspan="1">Class</td><td colspan="1">InstanceSpecification</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h4><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="55b6d176-05eb-4bf4-aca8-b9bc3d6ce51a">Changes types of Elements</ac:inline-comment-marker></h4><p>The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="1"><p>Element/Property</p></th><th><p>UAF 1.1 [Type]</p></th><th><p>UAF 1.2 [Type]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">EnterpriseGoal::benefit</span></td><td colspan="1">String</td><td colspan="1">ValueItem</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565467 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06489: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `249565467`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565467/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:54:17.193+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Enduring Task removed

The Enduring Task element was removed, so it became the Strategic Phase after the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Capability For Task removed

Capability For Task was removed, so after the migration, Capability For Task will be changed to Phases relationship (reverse).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for properties of ActualEnterprisePhase

- Operational Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Operational Architecture element.
- Resource Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Resource Architecture element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Vision property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision
- Goal property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal
- **Concern**property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between *Actual Enterprise Phase* and Concern

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for properties of Desired Effect and Achieved Effect

- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect is removed.
- DesiredEffect:: achievedEffect is removed.
- After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between **desires** target and **achieves** target if AchievedEffect:: **desiredEffect** and DesiredEffect::**achievedEffect** were linked with each other.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Consumes renamed

In UAF 1.2 Consumes relationship is renamed to Supports, and the direction is reversed.

##### Risk Owner removed

The Risk Owner property is removed, so after the migration, it will be added to the ToDo property in the Specification window of the Risk element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specializations of Capable Element updated

In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, the Exhibits relationship was removed, and Phases will be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes Property Types

In earlier versions, the benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2, the benefit property has a value Item for its value. After migration, a value item element will be created based on benefit value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for Whole Life Enterprise

In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

relationship will be created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Operational </em><em>Architecture</em> element.</li><li data-uuid="0550a0b0-6e7a-4fa2-b50f-a5dd5c46f220"><strong>Resource data-uuid="00fd077d-24cc-4606-a720-f049db7f8b19">AchievedEffect:: <strong>desiredEffect</strong> is removed.</li><li data-uuid="0dac3971-ec96-4d1c-a273-6ef47c0a9fd7">DesiredEffect::<strong>achievedEffect</strong> is removed.</li><li data-uuid="74a92532-e3f6-4565-a383-17365b272d85"><p>After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between <strong>desires</strong> target and <strong>achieves</strong> target element.</p><p><ac:image ac:height="243"><ri:attachment ri:filename="riskOwner_removed.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Specializations of Capable Element updated</h3><p>In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, the Exhibits relationship was removed, and Phases will be created. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exhibits_to_phases.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Changes Property Types</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In earlier versions, the benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2, the benefit property has a value Item for its value. After migration, a value item element will be created based on benefit value. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:height="234"><ri:attachment ri:filename="benefit_to_valueItem.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h3>Changes for Whole Life Enterprise</h3><p>In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase. </p><p><ac:image ac:height="86"><ri:attachment ri:filename="whole_life_enterprise_to_strategicPhase.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565463 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06490: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. New elements

- page_id: `249565463`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565463/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+New+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:48:29.159+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.2 | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Actual Effect | A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon. |
| Actual Outcome | Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect". |
| Capability Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic. |
| Challenge | An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals. |
| Challenge Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic. |
| Compares To | An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result. |
| Creates | A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset. |
| Driver | A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise. |
| Driver Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic. |
| Effect | A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes. |
| Enables | A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Enterprise Mission | Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision. |
| Enterprise Objective | A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals. |
| Evoked By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity. |
| Governed By | An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs. |
| Impacted By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity. |
| Maps To Goal | A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Motivated By | A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way. |
| Opportunity | An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges. |
| Owns Value | An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item. |
| Phases | An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. |
| Presented By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver. |
| Resource Service | A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services. |
| Resource Service Interface | A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize. |
| Security Risk | The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65]. |
| Sequence | A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another. |
| Service Architecture | An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective. |
| Service Contract | A constraint governing the use of one or more Services. |
| Service Control Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Service Exchange Item | An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange. |
| Service Exchange Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers. |
| Service Object Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Signal | A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Service Signal Property | A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal. |
| Strategic Constraint | A Rule governing a Capability. |
| Strategic Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Strategic Information | Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action. |
| Value Item | An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view. |
| Value Item Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time. |
| Value Stream | An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020]. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.</li><li>Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Service Object Flow</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal Property</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Constraint</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A Rule governing a Capability.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Exchange</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</span></td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Information</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value Item</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Value Item Kind</td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Value Stream</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020].</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565465 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06491: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Renamed and removed elements

- page_id: `249565465`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565465/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Renamed+and+removed+elements
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:49:50.794+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element UAF 1.1 [name] | Element UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Data Model Kind | Information Model Kind |
| Data Model | Information Model |
| Information Element | Operational Information |
| Data Element | Resource Information |
| Service Specification | Service |
| Consumes* | Supports |
| Achieved Effect | Achieves |
| Desired Effect | Desires |
| Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |
| Organization In Enterprise | Organization In Phase |

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Property UAF 1.1 [name] | Property UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Information Role | Operational Information Role |
| Data Role | Resource Information Role |
| Service Specification Role | Service Role |

****

**Removed properties:**

- Concern::enterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: resourceArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: operationalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern
- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect
- DesiredEffect::achievedEffect
- EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase
- EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase
- Risk::riskOwner

**Removed elements:**

- Enduring Task*
- Capability For Task

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

**New diagrams:**

| Name | Location |
| --- | --- |
|  | You can create it under the Strategic Processes. |
|  | You can create it under the Operational Processes. |
|  | You can create it under the Operational Processes. |
|  | You can create it under the Resources Processes. |
|  | You can create it under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g, Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters). |

| UAF 1.2 | UAF 1.3 |
| --- | --- |
| Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table | Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=253046134 space=MT version=23 -->
## PAGE 06494: UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `253046134`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/253046134/UAF+1.2+to+UAF+1.3.+Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 23
- version_date: `2026-01-16T14:04:53.748+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### UAF 1.2 Enterprise Mission to UAF 1.3 Actual Mission

###### In a single case

The Enterprise Mission is migrated to the Actual Mission, and the Strategic Phase is migrated to the Mission.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### In multiple cases

- The Enterprise Mission is migrated to the Actual Mission, and the Strategic Phase is migrated to the Mission [ATTACHMENT filename='enterprise_mission_to_actual_mission_multiple1.png']
- The Enterprise Mission is migrated to the Actual Mission, and the Strategic Phase is migrated to the Strategic Phase [ATTACHMENT filename='enterprise_mission_to_actual_mission_multiple2.png']

##### Location Holder

###### Asset

- The Asset was removed from the Location Holder subtypes.
- The requiredElement and physicalLocation information was added to the ToDo property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resource Role and Operational Role

- The requiredElement property type is changed from Actual Environment to Condition.
- The information from the requiredElement property was moved to the ToDo property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Phaseable element and Phases relationship

Changes of Phases relationship supplier Phaseable element:

- Opportunity was removed fromthe Phaseable Element subtypes.
- Motivational Element added to Phaseable Element subtypes (Opportunity is a subtype of Motivational Element.

Changes of Phases relationship client Actual Strategic Phase

- The Actual Strategic Phase derived property phasesOpportunity was renamed to phasesMotivationalElement .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Activity

- The ActivityPerformableUnderCondition property was removed
  - The property information was added to the ToDo property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Activity changes
  - Activity was removed from the Measureable element subtypes.
    - Properties MeasurementSet and ActualMeasurementSet are removed.
  - Activity was added as a subtype of a Property Set.
    - An Activity can own measurements directly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example 1. Measurement Set

Measurement is created for an Activity and typed by the Measurement Set (removed property value)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example 2. Measurement Set and Actual Measurement Set

- Measurement is created for an Activity and typed by the removed property Measurement Set.
- Actual Measurement is created for an Activity, and a new Actual Property Set is created with filled-in slots for the removed Actual Measurement Set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example 3. Actual Measurement Set

The information is added to the ToDo property if the Actual Measurement Set (removed property value) was without a type.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Standard and Actual Organization

ratifiedBy property:

- ratifiedBy property type was changed from Actual Organization to Actual Organizational Resource
- The property value after the migration is not affected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

-----------------------------

##### Modeling suggestion

Here is a suggestion on how to model with Opposable element and Designation Kind.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Mission.</p><p><ac:image ac:height="169"><ri:attachment ri:filename="enterprise_mission_to_actual_mission_single_case.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>In multiple cases</h4><ul><li data-uuid="a11288d2-f41d-48a5-9089-971e9d00f7bd">The Enterprise Mission is migrated to the Actual Mission, and the Strategic Phase is migrated to the Mission<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="enterprise_mission_to_actual_mission_multiple1.png" /></ac:image><br /><br />      </li><li data-uuid="af473c01-a14b-4366-b336-f98761276c28">The Enterprise Mission is migrated to the Actual Mission, and the Strategic Phase is migrated to the Strategic Phase<br /><ac:image ac:height="139"><ri:attachment ri:filename="enterprise_mission_to_actual_mission_multiple2.png" property.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_role_and_operational_role.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Phaseable element and Phases relationship</h3><p>Changes of Phases relationship supplier Phaseable element:</p><ul><li data-uuid="ed72f741-0a91-48da-90c6-a120f2feb76f">Opportunity was removed fromthe  Phaseable Element subtypes.</li><li to the ToDo property.</li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="activity_performableundercondition_todo.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="2f0cad62-9265-4ce9-8828-f7edd83f7eed">Activity changes<ul><li data-uuid="d5d0df65-f0db-490a-b44e-53f49768d283">Activity was removed from the Measureable element subtypes.<ul><li data-uuid="f4b4b3e9-6591-49a8-b477-4b6904600810">Properties MeasurementSet and ActualMeasurementSet are removed.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="805f9600-9533-43a1-a64b-6224e7c95de8">Activity was added as a subtype of a Property Set.<ul><li data-uuid="57e74b12-77f0-4fdf-8aae-69f06d9af124">An Activity can own measurements directly.</li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="activity_changes_main.png" /></ac:image></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Example 1. Measurement Set</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Measurement is created for an Activity and typed by the Measurement Set (removed property value)</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="example1_measurementset.png" /></ac:image></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Example 2. Measurement Set and Actual Measurement Set</h4><ul><li data-uuid="202fe39e-29f8-44b6-a530-07026b47c247">Measurement is created for an Activity and typed by the removed property Measurement Set.</li><li data-uuid="f5389acf-1ecd-4a72-9f0b-56d7ffb4bbc9">Actual Measurement is created for an Activity, and a new Actual Property Set is created with filled-in slots for the removed Actual Measurement Set.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="example2_measurementsetSet_actualMeasurementSet.png" /></ac:image></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Example 3. Actual Measurement Set</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">The information is added to the ToDo property if the Actual Measurement Set (removed property value) was without a type. </p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="example3_actualMeasurementSet.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Standard and Actual Organization</h3><p>ratifiedBy property:</p><ul><li data-uuid="30fe6eee-bca4-494d-8f15-c02329d9cc5b">ratifiedBy property type was changed from Actual Organization to Actual Organizational Resource</li><li data-uuid="ce65c195-3283-41cc-8cc4-fb00d875f4e3">The property value after the migration is not affected. </li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ratifiedBy.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>-----------------------------</p><h3>Modeling suggestion</h3><p>Here is a suggestion on how to model with Opposable element and Designation Kind.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modeling_example.png" /></ac:image></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=253046122 space=MT version=10 -->
## PAGE 06495: UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. New elements

- page_id: `253046122`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/253046122/UAF+1.2+to+UAF+1.3.+New+elements
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2025-10-28T16:14:55.061+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.3 | MetaClass | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Actual Mission | Instance Specification | A particular mission to accomplish the assigned mission objectives. |
| Actual Mission Phase | Instance Specification | A particular phase in the accomplishment of an overall actual mission. |
| Actual Mission Scenario | Instance Specification | A particular Mission Scenario within which an Actual Mission is to be accomplished. |
| Actual Mission Vignette | Instance Specification | A particular Mission Vignette within which an Actual Mission is to be accomplished. |
| Conflicts With | Comment | An annotation that indicates UAF elements acting against or in opposition to each other. This means that there are elements in the model that disagree about something important or are incompatible with each other in some way. |
| Defines | Abstraction | A tuple that links the StrategicPhase and the Activity that implements it. |
| Designation Kind | Enimeration | An enumerated type that defines the kind of structural element with respect to the purpose, intent, and capabilities of that element. |
| Doctrine | Class | A formal expression of military knowledge and thought, accepted as being relevant at a given time, which covers the nature of conflict, the preparation of the military forces for conflict, and the method of engaging in conflict to achieve success. it is descriptive rather than prescriptive, requiring judgment in application. |
| Mission | Class | The task, together with the purpose, that clearly indicates the action to be taken and the reasoning behind the mission. |
| Mission Engineering Thread | Activity | A sequence of end-to-end mission-related functions, actions, and events presented as a series of steps that implement a mission thread. |
| Mission Task | Activity | A clearly defined activity specifically to be assigned to a system, person, or organization that must be completed. |
| Mission Task Action | Call Behavior Action | The usage of a MissionTask within a MissionThread. |
| Mission Thread | Activity | A sequence of end-to-end mission tasks, activities, and events presented as a series of steps to achieve a mission. |
| Mission Scenario | Data Type | Description of the geographical location and time frame of the overall conflict. A scenario includes information such as threat and friendly politico-military contexts and backgrounds, assumptions, constraints, limitations, strategic objectives, and other planning considerations. |
| Mission Vignette | Data Type | Description of the geographical location and time frame of the overall conflict. A vignette includes information such as threat and friendly politico-military contexts and backgrounds, assumptions, constraints, limitations, strategic objectives, and other planning considerations. It can represent small, ideally self-contained parts of a scenario. |
| Opposes | Dependency | A tuple that indicates an open clash between two opposing groups (or individuals). This means that elements in the model are fighting against each other or are providing strong resistance to progress towards some goals. Note to entry: By individual, this does not indicate instances in the model. |
| Opposable Element | Element | A type of element that is categorized to allow for it to have an Opposes relationship to some other opposableelement |
| RuleKind: Assumption | Enumeration Literal | Indicates that the element is accepted as true or as certain to happen, without proof. |
| Mission Kind | Enumeration | An enumerated list of kinds of missions. Its enumeration literals are:National Policy - A broad course of action or statements of guidance adopted by the government at the national level in pursuit of national objectives.Theater Strategy - An overarching construct outlining a combatant commander’s vision for integrating and synchronizing military activities and operations with the other instruments of national power in order to achieve national strategic objectives. See also national military strategy; national securitystrategy; strategy.Campaign - A series of related major operations aimed at achieving strategic and operational objectives within a given time and space.Major Operation - A series of tactical actions (battles, engagements, strikes) conducted by combat forces, coordinated in time and place, to achieve strategic or operational objectives in an operational area.Battle - A hostile encounter between opposing military forces.Engagement - A tactical conflict, usually between opposing lower echelons maneuver forces. See also battle; campaign.Small Unit And Crew Action - Small unit and Crew Action is the combat deployment of platoons and smaller units in a particular strategic and logistic environment.Other - A different kind of mission. |
| ActualOrganizationalResource::ratifiedStandardsActualOrganizationalResource::publishedStandardsActualOrganizationalResource::createdStandards | Associations | ratifiedStandards : Standard [*] Standards that were ratified by this ActualOrganization.publishedStandards : Standard [*] A relationship between an actual organization and the standards published by that organizationcreatedStandards : Standard [*] A relationship between an actual organization and the standards created by that organization |
| Standard::publishedByStandard:: createdBy | Associations | publishedBy : ActualOrganizationalResource [*] An entity responsible for making the resource availablecreatedBy : ActualOrganizationalResource [*] The organization or creator of the standard or doctrine |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 82.4658%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 22.2068%;" /><col style="width: 10.7739%;" /><col style="width: 67.0193%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">UAF 1.3</th><th scope="col">MetaClass</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td>Actual Mission</td><td><p style="text-align: left;">Instance Specification</p></td><td><p style="text-align: left;">A particular mission to accomplish the assigned mission objectives.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Actual Mission Phase</span></td><td><p style="text-align: left;">Instance Specification</p></td><td><p style="text-align: left;">A particular phase in the accomplishment of an overall actual mission.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Actual Mission Scenario</span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Instance Specification</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">A particular Mission Scenario within which an Actual Mission is to be accomplished.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Actual Mission Vignette</span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Instance Specification</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">A particular Mission Vignette within which an Actual Mission is to be accomplished.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Conflicts With</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> Comment</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An annotation that indicates UAF elements acting against or in opposition to each other. This means that there are elements in the model that disagree about something important or are incompatible with each other in some way.</span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Defines</span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Abstraction</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">A tuple that links the StrategicPhase and the Activity that implements it.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Designation Kind</span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Enimeration</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">An enumerated type that defines the kind of structural element with respect to the purpose, intent, and capabilities of that element.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Doctrine</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> Class</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A formal expression of military knowledge and thought, accepted as being relevant at a given time, which covers the nature of conflict, the preparation of the military forces for conflict, and the method of engaging in conflict to achieve success. it is descriptive rather than prescriptive, requiring judgment in application.</span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Mission</span></td><td><p style="text-align: left;">Class</p></td><td><p style="text-align: left;">The task, together with the purpose, that clearly indicates the action to be taken and the reasoning behind the mission.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Mission Engineering Thread</td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Activity</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">A sequence of end-to-end mission-related functions, actions, and events presented as a series of steps that implement a mission thread.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Mission Task</span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Activity</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">A clearly defined activity specifically to be assigned to a system, person, or organization that must be completed.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Mission Task Action</span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Call Behavior Action</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">The usage of a MissionTask within a MissionThread.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Mission Thread</span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Activity</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">A sequence of end-to-end mission tasks, activities, and events presented as a series of steps to achieve a mission.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Mission Scenario</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> Data Type</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Description of the geographical location and time frame of the overall conflict. A scenario includes information such as threat and friendly politico-military contexts and backgrounds, assumptions, constraints, limitations, strategic objectives, and other planning considerations.</span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Mission Vignette</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> Data Type</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Description of the geographical location and time frame of the overall conflict. A vignette includes information such as threat and friendly politico-military contexts and backgrounds, assumptions, constraints, limitations, strategic objectives, and other planning considerations. It can represent small, ideally self-contained parts of a scenario.</span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Opposes</span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Dependency</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">A tuple that indicates an open clash between two opposing groups (or individuals). This means that elements in the model are fighting against each other or are providing strong resistance to progress towards some goals. Note to entry: By individual, this does not indicate instances in the model.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Opposable Element</span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">Element</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title=""><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="733ba544-4233-45c0-acd4-389198e26063">A type of element that is categorized to allow for it to have an Opposes relationship to some other opposable<br />element</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">RuleKind: Assumption</span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">Enumeration Literal</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">Indicates that the element is accepted as true or as certain to happen, without proof.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="512f5466-a03d-4e44-91d8-3c415113eb08">Mission Kind</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">Enumeration</p></td><td><p style="text-align: left;">An enumerated list of kinds of missions. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="9356463f-74ce-4e52-95ac-21f50303ef61">National Policy - A broad course of action or statements of guidance adopted by the government at the national level in pursuit of national objectives.</li><li class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="4fbad360-28b1-4ae9-860e-22fd60cda600">Theater Strategy - An overarching construct outlining a combatant commander’s vision for integrating and synchronizing military activities and operations with the other instruments of national power in order to achieve national strategic objectives. See also national military strategy; national security<br />strategy; strategy.</li><li class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="a30930a4-fba7-48a8-a49c-7d3a9db47787">Campaign - A series of related major operations aimed at achieving strategic and operational objectives within a given time and space.</li><li class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="ed304180-0aa0-4079-92fc-ea1fbda1d05d">Major Operation - A series of tactical actions (battles, engagements, strikes) conducted by combat forces, coordinated in time and place, to achieve strategic or operational objectives in an operational area.</li><li class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="b19f3826-0755-4c04-b561-a549ad164574">Battle - A hostile encounter between opposing military forces.</li><li class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="e813a228-176b-4df4-815a-61f76972febc">Engagement - A tactical conflict, usually between opposing lower echelons maneuver forces. See also battle; campaign.</li><li class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="a45be974-af3b-440f-9be6-3474e5c6c845">Small Unit And Crew Action - Small unit and Crew Action is the combat deployment of platoons and smaller units in a particular strategic and logistic environment.</li><li class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="e991b368-2b92-4748-b1d6-9ceafee4bc68">Other - A different kind of mission.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">ActualOrganizationalResource::ratifiedStandards</span></p><p title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">ActualOrganizationalResource::publishedStandards</span></p><p title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">ActualOrganizationalResource::createdStandards</span></p><p title=""><br /></p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">Associations</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">ratifiedStandards : Standard [*] Standards that were ratified by this ActualOrganization.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">publishedStandards : Standard [*] A relationship between an actual organization and the standards published by that organization</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">createdStandards : Standard [*] A relationship between an actual organization and the standards created by that organization</p></td></tr><tr><td><p title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Standard::publishedBy</span></p><p title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Standard:: createdBy</span></p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">Associations</p></td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">publishedBy : ActualOrganizationalResource [*] An entity responsible for making the resource available</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;" title="">createdBy : ActualOrganizationalResource [*] The organization or creator of the standard or doctrine</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563829 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06497: UAF 1.3

- page_id: `249563829`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563829/UAF+1.3
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:44:20.038+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Unified Architecture Framework (UAF) supports the capability to:

- model architectures for a broad range of complex systems, which may include hardware, software, data, personnel, and facility elements;
- model consistent architectures for system-of-systems (SoS) down to lower levels of design and implementation;
- support the analysis, specification, design, and verification of complex systems; and
- improve the ability to exchange architecture information among related tools that are SysML v1-based and tools that are based on other standards.

According to modeling needs, there are the following UAF templates for different purposes:

- UAF Project is designed to model architectures for a broad range of complex systems. The UAF provides an applicable model security controls, threat, risk, and risk mitigation. It allows for defining consistent architectures for System-of-Systems (SoS), including all creation phases from the design until implementation.
- EA with BPMN Project* - u sing this template, you will get a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.
- **UAF Project Template with Process Guide** - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build architecture by providing summary information about the UAF structure and architecture creation process.
- **EA Project Template with Process Guide** - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with the Enterprise Architecture Guide for UAF (a non-normative component of the UAF specification). This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.
- **Blank UAF Project** - creates a blank UAF project.

Please make sure, that the UAF 1.3 Plugin is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='IL'].

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

****

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### UAF 1.3 Grid. The grid is a way of showing how the various view specifications (cells) correspond to viewpoints (prev. known as domains) (horizontal rows) and the aspects (prev. known as model kinds) (the columns) that describe the view specification.

The descriptions of all the viewpoints are provided in the following table:

| Viewpoint | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Architecture Management | Am | Identifies the metadata required to develop a suitable architecture that is fit for its purpose. |
| Strategic | St | Capability management process. Describes the capability taxonomy, composition, dependencies, and evolution. |
| Operational | Op | Illustrates the Logical Architecture of the enterprise. Describes the requirements, operational behavior, structure, and exchanges required to support (exhibit) capabilities. Defines all operational elements in an implementation/solution-independent manner. |
| Services | Sv | The Service-Orientated View (SOV) is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service withinMODAF is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer.DoDAF: The Service Views within the Services Viewpoint describe the design for service-based solutions to support operational development processes (JCIDS) and Defense Acquisition System or capability development within the Joint Capability Areas. |
| Personnel | Ps | Defines and explores organizational resource types. Shows the taxonomy of types of organizational resources as well as connections, interaction, and growth over time. |
| Resources | Rs | Captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g., organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, and natural resources that implement the operational requirements. Further design of a resource is typically detailed in SysML v1 or UML. |
| Security | Sc | Security assets and security enclaves. Defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners, security constraints (policy, laws, and guidance), and details where they are located (security enclaves). |
| Projects | Pj | Describes projects and project milestones, how those projects deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to the projects, and dependencies between projects. |
| Standards | Sd | MODAF: Technical Standards Views are extended from the core DoDAF views to include non-technical standards such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc.DoDAF: The Standards Views within the Standards Viewpoint are the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of solution parts or elements. |
| Actual Resources | Ar | The analysis, e.g., evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations. Illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations. |

The descriptions of all the aspects are provided in the following table:

| Aspect | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Motivation | Mv | Captures motivational elements e.g., challenges, opportunities, and concerns, that pertain to enterprise transformation efforts, and different types of requirements, e.g., operational, services, personnel, resources, or security controls. |
| Taxonomy | Tx | Presents all the elements as a standalone structure. Presents all the elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element. |
| Structure | Sr | Describes the breakdown of structural elements e.g. logical performers, systems, projects, etc. into their smaller parts. |
| Connectivity | Cn | Describes the connections, relationships, and interactions between the different elements. |
| Processes | Pr | Captures activity-based behavior and flows. It describes activities, their Inputs/Outputs, activity actions, and flows between them. |
| States | St | Captures state-based behavior of an element. It is a graphical representation of states of a structural element and how it responds to various events and actions. |
| Sequences | Sq | Expresses a time-ordered examination of the exchanges as a result of a particular scenario. Provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating elements as a result of a particular scenario. |
| Information | If | Address the information perspective on operational, service, and resource architectures. Allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues. |
| Parameters | Pm | Shows the measurable properties of something in the physical world and elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept or set of systems. |
| Constraints | Ct | Details the measurements that set performance requirements constraining capabilities. Also defines the rules governing behavior and structure. |
| Roadmap | Rm | Addresses how elements in the architecture change over time. |
| Traceability | Tr | Describes the mapping between elements in the architecture. This can be between different viewpoints within domains as well as between domains. It can also be between structure and behaviors. |

**UAF 1.3 viewpoints**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
